<script type="application/ld+json"> 4: { 5: "@context": "https://schema.org", 6: "@type": "HowTo", 7: "name": "ast-grep Pattern Library for Code Consolidation", 8: "description": "This directory contains a comprehensive collection of ast-grep rules for detecting code patterns that are candidates for consolidation across repositories.", 9: "dateModified": "2026-01-19T02:09:57.563Z", 10: "inLanguage": "en-US" 11: } 12: </script> 13: 14: 15: This directory contains a comprehensive collection of ast-grep rules for detecting code patterns that are candidates for consolidation across repositories. 16: 17: ## Directory Structure 18: 19: ``` 20: .ast-grep/ 21: ├── sgconfig.yml # Master configuration 22: ├── duplicate-error-handling.yml # Legacy rule (moved to rules/) 23: ├── fs-operations.yml # Legacy rule (moved to rules/) 24: └── rules/ 25: ├── api/ # API and HTTP patterns 26: │ ├── auth-checks.yml 27: │ ├── error-responses.yml 28: │ ├── express-route-handlers.yml 29: │ └── request-validation.yml 30: ├── async/ # Asynchronous patterns 31: │ ├── await-patterns.yml 32: │ └── promise-chains.yml 33: ├── config/ # Configuration patterns 34: │ ├── config-objects.yml 35: │ └── env-variables.yml 36: ├── database/ # Database operation patterns 37: │ ├── connection-handling.yml 38: │ ├── prisma-operations.yml 39: │ └── query-builders.yml 40: ├── logging/ # Logging patterns 41: │ ├── console-statements.yml 42: │ └── logger-patterns.yml 43: └── utilities/ # Utility function patterns 44: ├── array-map-filter.yml 45: ├── object-manipulation.yml 46: ├── string-manipulation.yml 47: ├── type-checking.yml 48: └── validation.yml 49: ``` 50: 51: **Total Rules:** 18 active rules across 6 categories 52: 53: ## Rule Categories 54: 55: ### 1. Utilities (5 rules) 56: 57: **Purpose:** Detect common utility operations that could be consolidated 58: 59: - **array-map-filter.yml** - Array iteration methods (map, filter, reduce, forEach, find, findIndex) 60: - **object-manipulation.yml** - Object operations (keys, values, entries, assign, spread, JSON) 61: - **string-manipulation.yml** - String operations (trim, case, split, replace, slice, substring) 62: - **type-checking.yml** - Type checking patterns (typeof, instanceof, Array.isArray) 63: - **validation.yml** - Validation logic (null checks, error throws, early returns) 64: 65: ### 2. API Patterns (4 rules) 66: 67: **Purpose:** Identify API-related patterns for standardization 68: 69: - **express-route-handlers.yml** - Route handler patterns (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) 70: - **auth-checks.yml** - Authentication/authorization patterns (401/403 responses, token checks) 71: - **error-responses.yml** - API error response patterns (status codes, error objects) 72: - **request-validation.yml** - Request validation patterns (body, params, query validation) 73: 74: ### 3. Database (3 rules) 75: 76: **Purpose:** Track database access patterns for repository pattern consolidation 77: 78: - **prisma-operations.yml** - Prisma ORM patterns (findUnique, create, update, delete, etc.) 79: - **query-builders.yml** - Raw SQL query patterns (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) 80: - **connection-handling.yml** - Connection management (connect, disconnect, pooling) 81: 82: ### 4. Configuration (2 rules) 83: 84: **Purpose:** Find configuration access that should be centralized 85: 86: - **env-variables.yml** - Direct process.env access (should use config module) 87: - **config-objects.yml** - Configuration object patterns 88: 89: ### 5. Async Patterns (2 rules) 90: 91: **Purpose:** Detect asynchronous code patterns for consistency 92: 93: - **await-patterns.yml** - Async/await with try-catch patterns 94: - **promise-chains.yml** - Promise chains (.then, .catch, Promise.all, etc.) 95: 96: ### 6. Logging (2 rules) 97: 98: **Purpose:** Track logging patterns for standardization 99: 100: - **console-statements.yml** - Console.log usage (should be replaced with structured logger) 101: - **logger-patterns.yml** - Structured logger usage patterns 102: 103: ## Usage 104: 105: ### Scan a Single Directory 106: 107: ```bash 108: # Scan with all rules 109: ast-grep scan sidequest/ 110: 111: # Scan with specific rule 112: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/config/env-variables.yml sidequest/ 113: 114: # Scan multiple directories 115: ast-grep scan sidequest/ sidequest/output/condense/ directory-scan-reports/ 116: ``` 117: 118: ### Scan by Category 119: 120: ```bash 121: # Scan for all utility patterns 122: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/utilities/ sidequest/ 123: 124: # Scan for all API patterns 125: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/api/ sidequest/ 126: 127: # Scan for all database patterns 128: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/database/ sidequest/ 129: ``` 130: 131: ### Output Formats 132: 133: ```bash 134: # JSON output (for programmatic processing) 135: ast-grep scan --json sidequest/ 136: 137: # Default pretty output 138: ast-grep scan sidequest/ 139: 140: # Count matches only 141: ast-grep scan sidequest/ | grep -c "pattern" 142: ``` 143: 144: ## Test Results (on sidequest/ directory) 145: 146: | Rule Category | Matches Found | Notes | 147: |---------------|---------------|-------| 148: | Logger patterns | 3,405 | Very high usage of structured logging ✅ | 149: | Object manipulation | ~50+ | JSON.stringify, Object.entries common | 150: | Env variables | ~20+ | Some direct process.env access found | 151: | Async/await | ~15+ | Good try-catch error handling patterns | 152: | Array operations | TBD | Need to test | 153: | File system ops | ~20+ | Many fs.readFile, fs.writeFile patterns | 154: 155: ## Integration with Duplicate Detection Pipeline 156: 157: ### Phase 1: Pattern Discovery 158: 1. Run all rules across target repositories 159: 2. Collect matches with file locations and line numbers 160: 3. Group by pattern type 161: 162: ### Phase 2: Similarity Analysis 163: 1. Extract AST nodes for each match 164: 2. Compare structural similarity 165: 3. Calculate consolidation scores 166: 167: ### Phase 3: Recommendations 168: 1. Generate consolidation suggestions 169: 2. Prioritize by frequency and impact 170: 3. Suggest abstraction tier (local util, shared package, MCP, agent) 171: 172: ## Adding New Rules 173: 174: ### Rule Template 175: 176: ```yaml 177: id: rule-name 178: language: javascript # or typescript, python, etc. 179: rule: 180: any: # or 'all', 'pattern', 'not', etc. 181: - pattern: $PATTERN1 182: - pattern: $PATTERN2 183: message: Description of what was found 184: severity: info # or warning, error 185: note: Additional context for consolidation analysis 186: ``` 187: 188: ### Best Practices 189: 190: 1. **Use descriptive IDs:** `category-specific-name` (e.g., `database-prisma-operations`) 191: 2. **Provide context:** Include helpful notes about consolidation potential 192: 3. **Test thoroughly:** Verify rule matches intended patterns without false positives 193: 4. **Use appropriate severity:** 194: - `info` - Pattern tracking for analysis 195: - `warning` - Anti-pattern or should be refactored 196: - `error` - Code that must be changed 197: 5. **Document expected matches:** Add examples in rule comments 198: 199: ### Testing New Rules 200: 201: ```bash 202: # Test rule syntax 203: ast-grep test .ast-grep/rules/your-rule.yml 204: 205: # Test against sample code 206: echo 'const x = process.env.FOO' | ast-grep -p 'process.env.$VAR' 207: 208: # Scan specific directory 209: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/your-rule.yml path/to/test/ 210: ``` 211: 212: ## Pattern Syntax Reference 213: 214: ### Meta Variables 215: 216: - `$VAR` - Matches a single AST node 217: - `$$$` - Matches zero or more nodes 218: - `$$$ARGS` - Named multi-node match 219: - `$_VAR` - Non-capturing (performance optimization) 220: 221: ### Matchers 222: 223: ```yaml 224: rule: 225: pattern: $EXACT_MATCH # Single pattern 226: any: [$PAT1, $PAT2] # Match any pattern (OR) 227: all: [$PAT1, $PAT2] # Match all patterns (AND) 228: not: { pattern: $PAT } # Negation 229: inside: { pattern: $CONTEXT } # Must be inside context 230: ``` 231: 232: ### Advanced Patterns 233: 234: ```yaml 235: # Match repeated variables (detect duplicates) 236: pattern: $A == $A 237: 238: # Match multi-line code blocks 239: pattern: | 240: if ($COND) { 241: $$$BODY 242: } 243: 244: # Match specific structures 245: pattern: | 246: class $CLASS { 247: $METHOD($$$ARGS) { 248: $$$BODY 249: } 250: } 251: ``` 252: 253: ## Next Steps 254: 255: 1. **Expand rule library:** 256: - React/Vue component patterns 257: - Test framework patterns 258: - Module import/export patterns 259: 260: 2. **Create test suite:** 261: - Sample code for each pattern 262: - Expected match counts 263: - Automated rule validation 264: 265: 3. **Build aggregation tool:** 266: - Collect all scan results 267: - Deduplicate matches 268: - Generate consolidated reports 269: 270: 4. **Integrate with pydantic:** 271: - Define data models for matches 272: - Structure results for analysis 273: - Enable pipeline processing 274: 275: ## Resources 276: 277: - [ast-grep Documentation](https://ast-grep.github.io/) 278: - [Pattern Syntax Guide](https://ast-grep.github.io/guide/pattern-syntax.html) 279: - [Rule Configuration](https://ast-grep.github.io/guide/rule-config.html) 280: - [Playground](https://ast-grep.github.io/playground.html) 281: 282: --- 283: 284: **Created:** 2025-11-11 285: **Last Updated:** 2025-11-23 286: **Version:** 1.0.01: ruleDirs: 2: - rules 3: testConfigs: 4: - testDir: tests 5: outputFormat: json 1: name: CI - Tests and Checks 2: on: 3: pull_request: 4: branches: [main] 5: push: 6: branches: [main] 7: jobs: 8: test: 9: name: Run Tests 10: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 11: strategy: 12: matrix: 13: node-version: ['24.x'] 14: python-version: ['3.11', '3.12'] 15: steps: 16: - name: Checkout code 17: uses: actions/checkout@v4 18: - name: Set up pnpm 19: uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4 20: with: 21: version: 9 22: - name: Set up Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }} 23: uses: actions/setup-node@v4 24: with: 25: node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }} 26: cache: 'pnpm' 27: - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }} 28: uses: actions/setup-python@v5 29: with: 30: python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }} 31: cache: 'pip' 32: - name: Install Node.js dependencies 33: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 34: - name: Install ast-grep (best effort) 35: run: pnpm add -g @ast-grep/cli@latest || echo "⚠️ ast-grep installation failed (non-critical)" 36: continue-on-error: true 37: - name: Install Python dependencies 38: run: | 39: python -m pip install --upgrade pip 40: pip install -r requirements.txt 41: - name: Verify setup 47: run: pnpm run verify 48: - name: Run TypeScript type checking 49: run: pnpm run typecheck 50: - name: Run unit tests 51: run: pnpm test 52: - name: Run integration tests 53: env: 54: NODE_ENV: test 55: CI: true 56: run: pnpm run test:integration 57: continue-on-error: true 58: - name: Install dependencies 128: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 129: - name: Install ast-grep (best effort) 130: run: pnpm add -g @ast-grep/cli@latest || echo "⚠️ ast-grep installation failed (non-critical)" 131: continue-on-error: true 132: - name: Validate static assets 133: run: | 134: if [ -d public ]; then 135: grep -q "AlephAuto Dashboard" public/index.html || (echo "❌ Dashboard title not found" && exit 1) 136: SIZE=$(stat -f%z public/dashboard.css 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s public/dashboard.css) 137: if [ $SIZE -lt 1000 ]; then 138: echo "❌ CSS file too small: $SIZE bytes" 139: exit 1 140: fi 141: SIZE=$(stat -f%z public/dashboard.js 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s public/dashboard.js) 142: if [ $SIZE -lt 5000 ]; then 143: echo "❌ JS file too small: $SIZE bytes" 144: exit 1 145: fi 146: echo "✅ Static assets validated (public/)" 147: elif [ -d frontend ]; then 148: echo "✅ Dashboard uses frontend/ (build validated via pnpm run build:frontend)" 149: else 150: echo "⚠️ No dashboard directory found (skipping static asset validation)" 151: fi 152: - name: Test API server starts 153: run: | 154: timeout 30 node --strip-types api/server.ts & 155: SERVER_PID=$! 156: echo "Waiting for server to start..." 157: for i in {1..15}; do 158: if curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then 159: echo "✅ Server ready after ${i} seconds" 160: break 161: fi 162: if [ $i -eq 15 ]; then 163: echo "❌ Server failed to start within 15 seconds" 164: kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true 165: exit 1 166: fi 167: sleep 1 168: done 169: curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || (echo "❌ Health check failed" && exit 1) 170: kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true 171: echo "✅ API server validated" 172: env: 173: NODE_ENV: test 174: JOBS_API_PORT: 8080 1: name: CD - name: Set up pnpm 13: uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4 14: with: 15: version: 9 16: - name: Set up Node.js 17: uses: actions/setup-node@v4 18: with: 19: node-version: '20.x' 20: cache: 'pnpm' 21: - name: Set up Python 22: uses: actions/setup-python@v5 23: with: 24: python-version: '3.11' 25: cache: 'pip' 26: - name: Install Node.js dependencies 27: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod 28: - name: Install Python dependencies 29: run: | 30: python -m pip install --upgrade pip 31: pip install -r requirements.txt 32: - name: Install Doppler CLI 33: run: | 34: curl -sLf --retry 3 --tlsv1.2 --proto "=https" 'https://packages.doppler.com/public/cli/gpg.DE2A7741.key' | sudo apt-key add - 35: echo "deb https://packages.doppler.com/public/cli/deb/debian any-version main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/doppler-cli.list 36: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install doppler 37: - name: Install PM2 globally 38: run: pnpm add -g pm2 39: - name: Validate deployment files 40: run: | 41: echo "Validating deployment artifacts..." 42: test -f api/server.ts || test -f api/server.js || (echo "❌ API server not found" && exit 1) 43: grep -q '"dashboard"' package.json || (echo "❌ Dashboard script not found" && exit 1) 44: echo "✅ All deployment files validated" 45: - name: Configure Doppler 46: run: | 47: echo "${{ secrets.DOPPLER_TOKEN }}" | doppler configure set token --scope / --silent 48: env: 49: DOPPLER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOPPLER_TOKEN }} 50: - name: Deploy with rsync 51: uses: burnett01/rsync-deployments@6.0.0 52: with: 53: switches: -avzr --delete --exclude='.git*' --exclude='node_modules' --exclude='venv' --exclude='tests' 54: path: ./ 55: remote_path: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 56: remote_host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 57: remote_user: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 58: remote_key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 59: - name: Install dependencies on server 60: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 61: with: 62: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 63: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 64: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 65: script: | 66: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 67: mkdir -p logs 68: command -v pnpm || npm install -g pnpm 69: doppler run -c prd -- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod 70: python3 -m venv venv 71: source venv/bin/activate 72: pip install --upgrade pip 73: pip install -r requirements.txt 74: - name: Restart services with PM2 75: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 76: with: 77: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 78: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 79: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 80: script: | 81: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 82: if pm2 list | grep -q "aleph-dashboard\|aleph-worker"; then 83: echo "Services found, restarting with config/ecosystem.config.cjs..." 84: doppler run -c prd -- pm2 restart config/ecosystem.config.cjs 85: else 86: echo "Services not found, starting with config/ecosystem.config.cjs..." 87: doppler run -c prd -- pm2 start config/ecosystem.config.cjs 88: fi 89: pm2 save 90: pm2 status 91: - name: Health check 92: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 93: with: 94: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 95: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 96: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 97: script: | 98: echo "Waiting for services to start..." 99: sleep 10 100: if redis-cli ping | grep -q "PONG"; then 101: echo "✅ Redis is healthy" 102: else 103: echo "⚠️ Redis not available (caching disabled)" 104: fi 105: RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/health) 106: if [ "$RESPONSE" -eq 200 ]; then 107: echo "✅ Dashboard is healthy" 108: else 109: echo "❌ Dashboard health check failed (HTTP $RESPONSE)" 110: exit 1 111: fi 112: RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/api/health/doppler) 113: if [ "$RESPONSE" -eq 200 ]; then 114: echo "✅ Doppler health monitor is active" 115: else 116: echo "❌ Doppler health monitor check failed (HTTP $RESPONSE)" 117: exit 1 118: fi 119: PIPELINE_COUNT=$(curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/status | jq '.pipelines | length') 120: if [ "$PIPELINE_COUNT" -ge 7 ]; then 121: echo "✅ Dashboard returns $PIPELINE_COUNT pipelines (E1 verified)" 122: else 123: echo "❌ Dashboard only returns $PIPELINE_COUNT pipelines, expected >= 7" 124: exit 1 125: fi 126: REPOMIX_RESPONSE=$(curl -s 'http://localhost:8080/api/sidequest/pipeline-runners/repomix/jobs?limit=10') 127: REPOMIX_STATUS=$(echo $REPOMIX_RESPONSE | jq -r '.total // "skip"') 128: if [ "$REPOMIX_STATUS" != "skip" ] && [ "$REPOMIX_STATUS" != "null" ]; then 129: echo "✅ Pagination endpoint working (E2 verified)" 130: else 131: echo "⚠️ Pagination endpoint returned no data (may be normal for new deployment)" 132: fi 133: - name: Notify deployment success 134: if: success() 135: run: | 136: echo "🚀 Deployment successful!" 137: echo "📊 Dashboard: http://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:8080/" 138: echo "❤️ Health: http://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:8080/health" 139: - name: Rollback on failure 140: if: failure() 141: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 142: with: 143: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 144: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 145: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 146: script: | 147: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 148: echo "⚠️ Deployment failed, attempting rollback..." 149: pm2 resurrect 150: echo "Rollback complete. port = startPort; port <= endPort; port++) { 40: const available = await isPortAvailable(port, host); 41: if (available) { 42: logger.info({ port, host }, 'Found available port'); 43: return port; 44: } 45: } 46: logger.error({ startPort, endPort, host }, 'No available ports found in range'); 47: return null; 48: } 49: export async function killProcessOnPort(port: number): Promise<boolean> { 50: logger.info({ port, pids }, 'Killing processes on port'); 61: for (const pid of pids) { 62: await execAsync(`kill -9 ${pid}`); 63: logger.info({ port, pid }, 'Killed process'); 64: } 65: await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, PORT.RELEASE_DELAY_MS)); 66: return true; 67: } catch (error) { 68: logger.error({ port, error: (error as Error).message }, 'Failed to kill process on port'); 69: return false; 70: } 71: } 72: export function setupGracefulShutdown(httpServer: HttpServer, options: GracefulShutdownOptions = {}): void { 73: const { 74: onShutdown = () => {}, 75: timeout = PORT.DEFAULT_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS 76: } = options; 77: let isShuttingDown = false; 78: const shutdown = async (signal: string) => { 79: if (isShuttingDown) { 80: logger.warn({ signal }, 'Shutdown already in progress'); 81: return; 82: } 83: isShuttingDown = true; 84: logger.info({ signal }, 'Graceful shutdown initiated'); 85: const shutdownTimeout = setTimeout(() => { 86: logger.error({ timeout }, 'Shutdown timeout exceeded, forcing exit'); 87: process.exit(1); 88: }, timeout); 89: try { 90: await onShutdown(signal); 91: httpServer.close((err) => { 92: clearTimeout(shutdownTimeout); 93: if (err) { 94: logger.error({ error: err.message }, 'Error during HTTP server shutdown'); 95: process.exit(1); 96: } else { 97: logger.info('HTTP server closed successfully'); 98: process.exit(0); 99: } 100: }); 101: httpServer.closeAllConnections?.(); 102: } catch (error) { 103: clearTimeout(shutdownTimeout); 104: logger.error({ error: (error as Error).message }, 'Error during graceful shutdown'); 105: process.exit(1); 106: } 107: }; 108: process.on('SIGTERM', () => shutdown('SIGTERM')); 109: process.on('SIGINT', () => shutdown('SIGINT')); 110: process.on('SIGHUP', () => shutdown('SIGHUP')); 111: process.on('uncaughtException', (error) => { 112: logger.error({ error: error.message, stack: error.stack }, 'Uncaught exception'); 113: shutdown('uncaughtException'); 114: }); 115: process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, promise) => { 116: logger.error({ reason, promise }, 'Unhandled promise rejection'); 117: shutdown('unhandledRejection'); 118: }); 119: logger.info('Graceful shutdown handlers registered'); 120: } 121: export async function setupServerWithPortFallback(httpServer: HttpServer, options: PortFallbackOptions): Promise<number> { 122: const { 123: preferredPort, 124: maxPort = preferredPort + 10, 125: host = '0.0.0.0', 126: killExisting = false 127: } = options; 128: if (!preferredPort) { 129: throw new Error('preferredPort is required'); 130: } 131: logger.info({ preferredPort, maxPort, host, killExisting }, 'Setting up server with port fallback'); 132: const preferredAvailable = await isPortAvailable(preferredPort, host); 133: if (preferredAvailable) { 134: return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { 135: httpServer.listen(preferredPort, host, () => { 136: logger.info({ port: preferredPort, host }, 'Server listening on preferred port'); 137: resolve(preferredPort); 138: }); 139: httpServer.on('error', reject); 140: }); 141: } 142: logger.warn({ port: preferredPort }, 'Preferred port is not available'); 143: if logger.info({ port: preferredPort, host }, 'Server listening on preferred port after cleanup'); 152: resolve(preferredPort); 153: }); 154: httpServer.on('error', reject); 155: }); 156: } 157: } 158: } 159: const fallbackPort = await findAvailablePort(preferredPort + 1, maxPort, host); 160: if (!fallbackPort) { 161: throw new Error(`No available ports found between ${preferredPort} and ${maxPort}`); 162: } 163: logger.warn({ preferredPort, fallbackPort }, 'Using fallback port'); 164: return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { 165: httpServer.listen(fallbackPort, host, () => { 166: logger.info({ 68: }); 69: const heartbeat = setInterval(() => { 70: if (ws.readyState === ws.OPEN) { 71: ws.ping(); 72: } else { 73: clearInterval(heartbeat); 74: } 75: }, WEBSOCKET.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS); 76: ws.on('pong', () => { 77: logger.debug({ clientId }, 'Heartbeat received'); 78: }); 79: }); 80: wss.on('error', (error) => { 81: logError(logger, error, 'WebSocket server error'); 82: }); 83: wss.broadcast = (message: Record<string, unknown>, filter: ((client: WsClient, clientId: string) => boolean) | null = null) => { 84: const data = JSON.stringify(message); 103: if (!client) { 104: logger.warn({ clientId }, 'Client not found for targeted send'); 105: return false; 106: } 107: if (client.ws.readyState === client.ws.OPEN) { 108: client.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message)); 109: return true; 110: } 111: return false; 112: }; 113: wss.getClientInfo = (): { total_clients: number; clients: WsClientInfo[] } => { 114: return { 115: total_clients: clients.size, 116: clients: Array.from(clients.entries()).map(([id, client]) => ({ 117: client_id: id, 118: connected_at: client.connectedAt, 119: subscriptions: Array.from(client.subscriptions) 120: })) 121: }; 147: break; 148: case 'get_subscriptions': 149: client.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ 150: type: 'subscriptions', 151: subscriptions: Array.from(client.subscriptions), 152: timestamp: new Date().toISOString() 153: })); 154: break; 155: default: 156: logger.warn({ clientId, messageType: message.type }, 'Unknown message type'); 157: client.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ 158: type: 'error', 159: error: `Unknown message type: ${message.type}`, 160: timestamp: new Date().toISOString() 161: })); 55: this.send({ 56: type: 'subscribe', 57: channels: ['jobs', 'pipelines', 'system', 'activity', 'scans', '*'] 58: }); 59: break; 60: case 'subscribed': 61: console.log('[WebSocket] Subscribed to channels:', message.channels); 62: break; 63: case 'pong': 64: break; 65: case 'job:created': 66: this.handleJobCreated(message.data); 67: break; 68: case 'job:started': 69: this.handleJobStarted(message.data); 70: break; 71: case 'job:progress': 72: this.handleJobProgress(message.data); 134: } 135: private handleJobCreated(job: Job): void { 136: const store = useDashboardStore.getState(); 137: store.setQueuedJobs([...store.queuedJobs, job]); 138: this.addActivity({ 139: id: `activity-${job.id}-created`, 140: type: 'queued' as ActivityType, 141: pipelineId: job.pipelineId, 142: pipelineName: job.pipelineName || 'Unknown Pipeline', 143: message: `Job ${job.id.substring(0, 8)}... queued`, 144: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 145: jobId: job.id, 146: }); 165: } 166: private handleJobProgress(data: { jobId: string; progress: number; currentOperation?: string }): void { 167: const store = useDashboardStore.getState(); 168: store.updateJob(data.jobId, { 169: progress: data.progress, 170: currentOperation: data.currentOperation, 171: }); 172: } 173: private handleJobCompleted(job: Job): void { 174: const store = useDashboardStore.getState(); 175: store.setActiveJobs(store.activeJobs.filter((j) => j.id !== job.id)); 176: this.addActivity({ 177: id: `activity-${job.id}-completed`, 178: type: 'completed' as ActivityType, 179: pipelineId: job.pipelineId, 180: pipelineName: job.pipelineName || 'Unknown Pipeline', 181: message: `Job ${job.id.substring(0, 8)}... completed successfully`, 182: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 183: jobId: job.id, 184: severity: 'success', 185: details: job.results, 186: }); 187: } 188: private handleJobFailed(job: Job): void { 189: const store = useDashboardStore.getState(); 190: store.setActiveJobs(store.activeJobs.filter((j) => j.id !== job.id)); 191: store.updatePipeline(job.pipelineId, { 192: status: 'failed' as any, 193: lastError: job.error, 194: }); 195: this.addActivity({ 196: id: `activity-${job.id}-failed`, 197: type: 'failed' as ActivityType, 198: pipelineId: job.pipelineId, 199: pipelineName: job.pipelineName || 'Unknown Pipeline', 200: message: `Job ${job.id.substring(0, 8)}... failed: ${job.error || 'Unknown error'}`, 201: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 202: jobId: job.id, 203: severity: 'error', 204: details: { 205: error: job.error, 206: errorType: job.errorType, 207: }, 208: }); 209: } 210: private handleJobCancelled(job: Job): void { 211: const store = useDashboardStore.getState(); 212: store.removeJob(job.id); 213: this.addActivity({ 214: id: `activity-${job.id}-cancelled`, 215: type: 'cancelled' as ActivityType, 216: pipelineId: job.pipelineId, 217: pipelineName: job.pipelineName || 'Unknown Pipeline', 218: message: `Job ${job.id.substring(0, 8)}... cancelled`, 219: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 220: jobId: job.id, 221: severity: 'warning', 222: }); 223: } 224: private handlePipelineStatus(pipeline: Pipeline): void { 225: useDashboardStore.getState().updatePipeline(pipeline.id, pipeline); 226: } 227: private handleActivityEvent(activity: any): void { 228: const typeMapping: Record<string, ActivityType> = { 229: 'job:created': ActivityType.QUEUED, 230: 'job:started': ActivityType.STARTED, 231: 'job:completed': ActivityType.COMPLETED, 232: 'job:failed': ActivityType.FAILED, 233: 'job:cancelled': ActivityType.CANCELLED, 234: 'retry:created': ActivityType.STARTED, 235: 'retry:max-attempts': ActivityType.FAILED, 236: }; tailwindcss: {}, 4: autoprefixer: {}, 5: }, 6: } 1: # AlephAuto Dashboard Frontend 2: 3: Real-time monitoring dashboard for the AlephAuto job queue framework. 4: 5: ## Tech Stack 6: 7: - **React 18** - UI framework 8: - **TypeScript** - Type safety 9: - **Vite** - Build tool and dev server 10: - **Tailwind CSS** - Utility-first CSS framework 11: - **Zustand** - State management 12: - **Socket.IO Client** - Real-time WebSocket communication 13: - **Axios** - HTTP client for REST API 14: - **Recharts** - Data visualization 15: - **Lucide React** - Icon library 16: 17: ## Project Structure 18: 19: ``` 20: frontend/ 21: ├── src/ 22: │ ├── components/ # React components 23: │ ├── hooks/ # Custom React hooks 24: │ ├── services/ # API and WebSocket services 25: │ ├── store/ # Zustand state management 26: │ ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions 27: │ ├── App.tsx # Root component 28: │ ├── main.tsx # Entry point 29: │ └── index.css # Global styles (Tailwind) 30: ├── public/ # Static assets 31: ├── index.html # HTML entry point 32: ├── vite.config.ts # Vite configuration 33: ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript configuration 34: ├── tailwind.config.js # Tailwind CSS configuration 35: └── package.json # Dependencies and scripts 36: ``` 37: 38: ## Getting Started 39: 40: ### Prerequisites 41: 42: - Node.js >= 18.0.0 43: - npm or yarn 44: 45: ### Installation 46: 47: ```bash 48: cd frontend 49: npm install 50: ``` 51: 52: ### Development 53: 54: Start the development server: 55: 56: ```bash 57: npm run dev 58: ``` 59: 60: The dashboard will be available at `http://localhost:5173` 61: 62: The Vite dev server is configured to proxy API requests: 63: - `/api/*` → `http://localhost:8080` 64: - `/ws` (WebSocket) → `ws://localhost:8080` 65: 66: ### Build 67: 68: Create a production build: 69: 70: ```bash 71: npm run build 72: ``` 73: 74: Preview the production build: 75: 76: ```bash 77: npm run preview 78: ``` 79: 80: ### Type Checking 81: 82: Run TypeScript type checking without emitting files: 83: 84: ```bash 85: npm run typecheck 86: ``` 87: 88: ## Environment Variables 89: 90: Create a `.env.local` file for local development (optional): 91: 92: ```env 93: VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 94: VITE_WS_URL=ws://localhost:8080 95: ``` 96: 97: ## Implementation Status 98: 99: ### ✅ Completed 100: 101: - [x] Project setup and configuration 102: - [x] Dependencies installation 103: - [x] TypeScript configuration 104: - [x] Tailwind CSS configuration 105: - [x] Vite build configuration 106: - [x] Type definitions (Pipeline, Job, Activity, SystemStatus) 107: - [x] Basic project structure 108: 109: ### 🔄 In Progress 110: 111: - [ ] Zustand store implementation 112: - [ ] WebSocket service 113: - [ ] API service layer 114: - [ ] Core components (Header, Pipeline, JobQueue, Activity) 115: - [ ] Real-time data flow 116: 117: ### 📋 Planned 118: 119: - [ ] Documentation viewer component 120: - [ ] Responsive design testing 121: - [ ] Accessibility audit (WCAG AA) 122: - [ ] Performance optimization 123: - [ ] Production deployment 124: 125: ## Next Steps 126: 127: Following the implementation guide in `docs/DASHBOARD_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md`: 128: 129: 1. **Implement Zustand Store** (`src/store/dashboard.ts`) 130: - Pipeline state management 131: - Job queue state 132: - Activity feed state 133: - System status state 134: 135: 2. **Create WebSocket Service** (`src/services/websocket.ts`) 136: - Connection management with reconnection logic 137: - Event handlers for job lifecycle 138: - System status updates 139: 140: 3. **Build API Service** (`src/services/api.ts`) 141: - REST endpoints for pipelines, jobs, scans 142: - Error handling and retries 143: - Type-safe responses 144: 145: 4. **Create Core Components** 146: - Layout component (grid structure) 147: - Header (system status, settings) 148: - PipelineStatus (pipeline cards with progress) 149: - JobQueue (active and queued jobs) 150: - RecentActivity (activity feed) 151: 152: 5. **Connect Real-Time Updates** 153: - WebSocket integration hook 154: - Polling fallback for connection issues 155: - State synchronization 156: 157: ## Documentation 158: 159: Full design and implementation documentation available in: 160: - `docs/DASHBOARD_SUMMARY.md` - Executive overview 161: - `docs/DASHBOARD_UI_DESIGN.md` - Complete UI specification 162: - `docs/DASHBOARD_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md` - Technical implementation guide 163: - `docs/DASHBOARD_VISUAL_MOCKUPS.md` - Design tokens and mockups 164: - `docs/DASHBOARD_QUICK_REFERENCE.md` - Developer cheat sheet 165: 166: ## API Integration 167: 168: The dashboard connects to the AlephAuto backend API running on port 8080: 169: 170: **Health Check** 171: - `GET /health` - System health status 172: 173: **Pipelines** 174: - `GET /api/pipelines` - List all pipelines 175: - `GET /api/pipelines/:id` - Get pipeline details 176: 177: **Jobs** 178: - `GET /api/jobs?status=running` - Get active jobs 179: - `GET /api/jobs?status=queued` - Get queued jobs 180: - `GET /api/jobs/:id` - Get job details 181: - `GET /api/jobs/:id/logs` - Get job logs 182: - `POST /api/jobs/:id/cancel` - Cancel a job 183: - `POST /api/jobs/:id/retry` - Retry a failed job 184: 185: **Scans** 186: - `POST /api/scan` - Trigger a new scan 187: - `GET /api/scan/:id` - Get scan results 188: 189: ## Contributing 190: 191: When adding new features: 192: 1. Follow the existing project structure 193: 2. Add TypeScript types in `src/types/` 194: 3. Use Zustand for state management 195: 4. Follow Tailwind CSS utility patterns 196: 5. Run `npm run typecheck` before committing 197: 198: ## License 199: 200: ISC 1: export default { 2: content: [ 3: "./index.html", 4: "./src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}", 5: ], 6: darkMode: 'class', 7: theme: { 8: extend: { 9: colors: { 10: 'slate': { 11: 850: '#1e293b', 12: 950: '#0f172a', 13: }, 14: }, 15: animation: { 16: 'spin-slow': 'spin 2s linear infinite', 17: 'pulse-slow': 'pulse 3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite', 18: }, 19: }, 20: }, 21: plugins: [], 22: } 1: { 2: "compilerOptions": { 3: "target": "ES2020", 4: "useDefineForClassFields": true, 5: "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"], 6: "module": "ESNext", 7: "skipLibCheck": true, 8: 9: /* Bundler mode */ 10: "moduleResolution": "bundler", 11: "allowImportingTsExtensions": true, 12: "resolveJsonModule": true, 13: "isolatedModules": true, 14: "noEmit": true, 15: "jsx": "react-jsx", 16: 17: /* Linting */ 18: "strict": true, 19: "noUnusedLocals": true, 20: "noUnusedParameters": true, 21: "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true 22: }, 23: "include": ["src"], 24: "references": [{ "path": "./tsconfig.node.json" }] 25: } 1: { 2: "compilerOptions": { 3: "composite": true, 4: "skipLibCheck": true, 5: "module": "ESNext", 6: "moduleResolution": "bundler", 7: "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true 8: }, 9: "include": ["vite.config.ts"] 10: } 1: import { defineConfig } from 'vite'; 1: #!/usr/bin/env node 2: import { initDatabase, getAllJobs, saveJob } from '../sidequest/core/database.js'; 3: import { generateReport } from '../sidequest/utils/report-generator.ts'; 4: import { createComponentLogger, logError, logStart } from '../sidequest/utils/logger.js'; 5: const logger = createComponentLogger('RetroactiveReports'); 6: function getAllCompletedJobs() { 7: const rows = getAllJobs({ status: 'completed', limit: 1000 }); 8: return rows.map(row => ({ 9: id: row.id, 10: pipelineId: row.pipeline_id, 11: status: row.status, 12: createdAt: row.created_at, 13: startedAt: row.started_at, 14: completedAt: row.completed_at, 15: data: row.data ? (typeof row.data === 'string' ? JSON.parse(row.data) : row.data) : {}, 16: result: row.result ? (typeof row.result === 'string' ? JSON.parse(row.result) : row.result) : {}, 17: error: row.error ? (typeof row.error === 'string' ? JSON.parse(row.error) : row.error) : null 18: })); 19: } 20: function hasReport(job) { 21: if (job.result) { 22: if (job.result.reportPaths || job.result.reportPath || job.result.report_paths) { 23: return true; 24: } 25: } 26: return false; 27: } 28: function updateJobResult(jobId, job, reportPaths) { 29: const updatedResult = { 30: ...job.result, 31: reportPaths 32: }; 33: saveJob({ 34: id: jobId, 35: pipelineId: job.pipelineId, 36: status: job.status, 37: createdAt: job.createdAt, 38: startedAt: job.startedAt, 39: completedAt: job.completedAt, 40: data: job.data, 41: result: updatedResult, 42: error: job.error 43: }); 44: logger.info({ jobId, reportPaths }, 'Updated job result with report paths'); 45: } 46: async function main() { 47: try { 48: logStart(logger, 'retroactive report generation'); 49: await initDatabase(); 50: const jobs = getAllCompletedJobs(); 51: logger.info({ totalJobs: jobs.length }, 'Found completed jobs'); 52: const jobsNeedingReports = jobs.filter(job => !hasReport(job)); 53: logger.info({ jobsNeedingReports: jobsNeedingReports.length }, 'Jobs needing reports'); 54: if (jobsNeedingReports.length === 0) { 55: logger.info('No jobs need reports. All done!'); 56: return; 57: } 58: let successCount = 0; 59: let errorCount = 0; 60: for (const job of jobsNeedingReports) { 61: try { 62: logger.info({ jobId: job.id, pipelineId: job.pipelineId }, 'Generating report'); 63: const startTime = job.startedAt ? new Date(job.startedAt).getTime() : new Date(job.createdAt).getTime(); 64: const endTime = job.completedAt ? new Date(job.completedAt).getTime() : Date.now(); 65: const metadata = {}; 66: if (job.result) { 67: const metadataFields = [ 68: 'healthScore', 'criticalIssues', 'warnings', 69: 'reportType', 'filesGenerated', 70: 'totalRepositories', 'dryRun', 71: 'targetDir', 'totalItems', 72: 'repoName', 'outputSize', 73: 'schemaType', 'impactScore', 'rating' 74: ]; 75: for (const field of metadataFields) { 76: if (job.result[field] !== undefined) { 77: metadata[field] = job.result[field]; 78: } 79: } 80: } 81: const reportPaths = await generateReport({ 82: jobId: job.id, 83: jobType: job.pipelineId, 84: status: job.status, 85: result: job.result, 86: startTime, 87: endTime, 88: parameters: job.data, 89: metadata 90: }); 91: updateJobResult(job.id, job, reportPaths); 92: successCount++; 93: logger.info({ 94: jobId: job.id, 95: htmlReport: reportPaths.html 96: }, 'Report generated successfully'); 97: } catch (error) { 98: errorCount++; 99: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to generate report', { 100: jobId: job.id, 101: pipelineId: job.pipelineId 102: }); 103: } 104: } 105: logger.info({ 106: total: jobsNeedingReports.length, 107: success: successCount, 108: errors: errorCount 109: }, 'Report generation complete'); 110: console.log('\n📊 Summary:'); 111: console.log(` Total jobs processed: ${jobsNeedingReports.length}`); 112: console.log(` ✅ Successfully generated: ${successCount}`); 113: console.log(` ❌ Errors: ${errorCount}`); 114: console.log(`\n✨ Reports saved to: output/reports/\n`); 115: } catch (error) { 116: logError(logger, error, 'Fatal error in retroactive report generation'); 117: console.error('❌ Error:', error.message); 118: process.exit(1); 119: } 120: } 121: if (import.meta.url === `file://${process.argv[1]}`) { 122: main().catch(error => { 123: console.error('Fatal error:', error); 124: process.exit(1); 125: }); 126: } 127: export { main }; 1: #!/usr/bin/env node 2: import initSqlJs from 'sql.js'; 3: import fs from 'fs'; 4: import path from 'path'; 5: import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'; 6: const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url); 7: const __dirname = path.dirname(__filename); 8: const LOCAL_DB_PATH = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'data', 'jobs.db'); 9: const RENDER_API_URL = process.env.RENDER_API_URL || 'https://alephauto.onrender.com'; 10: const MIGRATION_API_KEY = process.env.MIGRATION_API_KEY; 11: const DRY_RUN = process.argv.includes('--dry-run'); 12: async function loadLocalDatabase() { 13: console.log(`📂 Loading local database from: ${LOCAL_DB_PATH}`); 14: if (!fs.existsSync(LOCAL_DB_PATH)) { 15: throw new Error(`Local database not found: ${LOCAL_DB_PATH}`); 16: } 17: const SQL = await initSqlJs(); 18: const buffer = fs.readFileSync(LOCAL_DB_PATH); 19: const db = new SQL.Database(buffer); 20: return db; 21: } 22: function extractJobs(db) { 23: console.log('📊 Extracting jobs from local database...'); 24: const result = db.exec(` 25: SELECT 26: id, 27: pipeline_id, 28: status, 29: created_at, 30: started_at, 31: completed_at, 32: data, 33: result, 34: error, 35: git 36: FROM jobs 37: ORDER BY created_at ASC 38: `); 39: if (!result.length || !result[0].values.length) { 40: return []; 41: } 42: const columns = result[0].columns; 43: const jobs = result[0].values.map(row => { 44: const job = {}; 45: columns.forEach((col, i) => { 46: job[col] = row[i]; 47: }); 48: return job; 49: }); 50: console.log(` Found ${jobs.length} jobs`); 51: const byPipeline = {}; 52: for (const job of jobs) { 53: const pid = job.pipeline_id || 'unknown'; 54: byPipeline[pid] = (byPipeline[pid] || 0) + 1; 55: } 56: console.log(' Jobs by pipeline:'); 57: for (const [pipeline, count] of Object.entries(byPipeline)) { 58: console.log(` - ${pipeline}: ${count}`); 59: } 60: return jobs; 61: } 62: async function checkRenderHealth() { 63: console.log(`\n🔍 Checking Render API health: ${RENDER_API_URL}`); 64: try { 65: const response = await fetch(`${RENDER_API_URL}/health`); 66: const data = await response.json(); 67: if (data.status === 'healthy') { 68: console.log(' ✅ Render API is healthy'); 69: return true; 70: } else { 71: console.log(` ⚠️ Render API status: ${data.status}`); 72: return false; 73: } 74: } catch (error) { 75: console.error(` ❌ Failed to connect to Render API: ${error.message}`); 76: return false; 77: } 78: } 79: async function checkExistingJobs() { 80: console.log('\n📋 Checking existing jobs on Render...'); 81: try { 82: const response = await fetch(`${RENDER_API_URL}/api/status`); 83: const data = await response.json(); 84: const totalJobs = data.pipelines?.reduce((sum, p) => 85: sum + (p.completedJobs || 0) + (p.failedJobs || 0), 0) || 0; 86: console.log(` Found ${totalJobs} existing jobs on Render`); 87: if (data.pipelines?.length) { 88: console.log(' Existing pipelines:'); 89: for (const pipeline of data.pipelines) { 90: console.log(` - ${pipeline.name}: ${pipeline.completedJobs || 0} completed, ${pipeline.failedJobs || 0} failed`); 91: } 92: } 93: return totalJobs; 94: } catch (error) { 95: console.error(` ⚠️ Could not check existing jobs: ${error.message}`); 96: return 0; 97: } 98: } 99: async function importJobsToRender(jobs) { 100: console.log(`\n📤 Importing ${jobs.length} jobs to Render...`); 101: if (DRY_RUN) { 102: console.log(' 🔸 DRY RUN - No changes will be made'); 103: console.log(' Would import:'); 104: for (const job of jobs.slice(0, 5)) { 105: console.log(` - ${job.id} (${job.pipeline_id}): ${job.status}`); 106: } 107: if (jobs.length > 5) { 108: console.log(` ... and ${jobs.length - 5} more`); 85: logger.info({ 86: repoPath, 87: fromCommit: fromCommit.substring(0, 7), 88: toCommit: currentCommit.substring(0, 7), 89: filesChanged: changedFiles.length 90: }, 'Retrieved changed files'); 91: return changedFiles; 92: } catch (error) { 93: logger.error({ repoPath, error }, 'Failed to get changed files'); 94: return []; 95: } 96: } 97: async getCommitMetadata(repoPath: string, commitHash = 'HEAD'): Promise<CommitMetadata | null> { 98: try { 99: const { stdout } = await execPromise( 100: `git show -s --format='%H|%an|%ae|%at|%s' ${commitHash}`, 101: { cwd: repoPath } 102: ); 103: const [hash, author, email, timestamp, message] = stdout.trim().split('|'); 104: const metadata: CommitMetadata = { 105: hash, 106: shortHash: hash.substring(0, 7), 107: author, 108: email, 109: date: new Date(parseInt(timestamp) * 1000).toISOString(), 110: message 111: }; 112: logger.debug({ repoPath, metadata }, 'Retrieved commit metadata'); 113: return metadata; 114: } catch (error) { 115: logger.error({ repoPath, commitHash, error }, 'Failed to get commit metadata'); 116: return null; 117: } 118: } 119: async getBranchName(repoPath: string): Promise<string | null> { 120: try { 121: const { stdout } = await execPromise('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', { 122: cwd: repoPath 123: }); 124: const branchName = stdout.trim(); 125: logger.debug({ repoPath, branchName }, 'Retrieved branch name'); 126: return branchName; 127: } catch (error) { 128: logger.warn({ repoPath, error: (error as Error).message }, 'Failed to get branch name'); 145: return false; 146: } 147: } 148: async getRemoteUrl(repoPath: string, remoteName = 'origin'): Promise<string | null> { 149: try { 150: const { stdout } = await execPromise(`git remote get-url ${remoteName}`, { 151: cwd: repoPath 152: }); 231: }); 232: logger.info({ 233: repoPath, 234: commitsRetrieved: commits.length 235: }, 'Retrieved commit history'); 236: return commits; 237: } catch (error) { 238: logger.error({ repoPath, error }, 'Failed to get commit history'); classifyByErrorCode(errorCode: string): ErrorClassification | null { 127: const config = ERROR_CODE_CONFIG[errorCode]; 128: if (!config) return null; 129: return { 130: category: config.retryable ? ''; 190: if (errorMessage) { 191: const messageClassification = classifyByMessagePattern(errorMessage); 192: if (messageClassification) return messageClassification; 193: } 194: // Priority 4: Default classification 195: return DEFAULT_CLASSIFICATION; 196: } 197: /** 198: * Check if http error. 199: * 200: * @param {Error} error - The error 201: * 202: * @returns {error is HTTPError} True if http error, False otherwise 203: */ 204: export function isHTTPError(error: Error): error is HTTPError { 205: return 'statusCode' in error || 'status' in error; 206: } 207: export function isClassifiedError(error: Error): error is ClassifiedError { 208: return 'classification' in error && 'retryable' in error; 209: } 210: export function isRetryable(error: Error): boolean { 211: return classifyError(error).category === ErrorCategory.RETRYABLE; 212: } 213: export interface ErrorInfo { 214: name: string; 215: message: string; 216: code: string | number | undefined; 217: statusCode: number | undefined; 218: category: string; 219: reason: string; 220: suggestedDelay: number; 221: retryable: boolean; 222: stack: string | undefined; 223: cause: unknown; 224: } 225: export function getErrorInfo(error: Error): ErrorInfo { 226: const classification = classifyError(error); 227: const extError = error as ExtendedError; 228: const httpError = error as HTTPError; 229: return { 230: name: error.name ?? 94: for (let i = 0; i < suggestionBatches.length; i++) { 95: const batch = suggestionBatches[i]; 96: try { 97: const prUrl = await this._createPRForBatch( 98: batch, 99: repositoryPath, 100: i + 1, 101: _options 102: ); 103: if (prUrl) { 104: results.prsCreated++; 105: results.prUrls.push(prUrl); 106: } else { 107: results.skipped += batch.length; 108: } 109: } catch (error) { 110: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to create PR for batch', { batch: i + 1 }); 111: results.errors.push({ 112: batch: i + 1, 113: error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error), 114: suggestions: batch.map(s => s.suggestion_id) 115: }); 116: Sentry.captureException(error, { 117: tags: { 118: component: 'pr-creator', 119: batch: i + 1 120: }, 121: extra: { 122: repositoryPath, 123: suggestions: batch.map(s => s.suggestion_id) 124: } 125: }); 126: } 127: } 128: logger.info(results, 'PR creation completed'); 129: return results; 130: } 131: private async _createPRForBatch( 132: suggestions: Suggestion[], 133: repositoryPath: string, 134: batchNumber: number, 135: _options: Record<string, unknown> 136: ): Promise<string | null> { 137: const branchName = this._generateBranchName(suggestions, batchNumber); 138: logger.info({ 139: branchName, 140: suggestions: suggestions.length, 141: batchNumber 142: }, 'Creating PR for suggestion batch'); 143: try { 144: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['checkout', this.baseBranch]); 145: if (!this.dryRun) { 146: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['pull', 'origin', this.baseBranch]); 147: } 148: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['checkout', '-b', branchName]); 149: const filesModified = await this._applySuggestions(suggestions, repositoryPath); 150: if (filesModified.length === 0) { 151: logger.warn({ branchName }, 'No files were modified, skipping PR creation'); 152: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['checkout', this.baseBranch]); 153: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['branch', '-D', branchName]); 154: return null; 155: } 156: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['add', '.']); 157: const commitMessage = this._generateCommitMessage(suggestions, filesModified); 158: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['commit', '-m', commitMessage]); 159: if (this.dryRun) { 160: logger.info({ branchName }, 'Dry run: Skipping push and PR creation'); 161: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['checkout', this.baseBranch]); 162: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['branch', '-D', branchName]); 163: return `dry-run-${branchName}`; 164: } 165: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['push', '-u', 'origin', branchName]); 166: const prDescription = this._generatePRDescription(suggestions, filesModified); 167: const prTitle = this._generatePRTitle(suggestions, batchNumber); 168: const prUrl = await this._createPR(repositoryPath, branchName, prTitle, prDescription); 169: logger.info({ prUrl, branchName }, 'PR created successfully'); 170: return prUrl; 171: } catch (error) { 172: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to create PR', { branchName }); 173: try { 174: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['checkout', this.baseBranch]); 175: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['branch', '-D', branchName]); 176: } catch (cleanupError) { 177: logger.warn({ error: cleanupError }, 'Failed to cleanup branch'); 178: } 179: throw error; 180: } 181: } 182: private async _applySuggestions(suggestions: Suggestion[], repositoryPath: string): Promise<string[]> { 183: const filesModified: string[] = []; 184: for (const suggestion of suggestions) { 185: try { 186: if (suggestion.target_location && suggestion.proposed_implementation) { 187: const targetPath = path.join(repositoryPath, suggestion.target_location); 188: await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(targetPath), { recursive: true }); 189: await fs.writeFile(targetPath, suggestion.proposed_implementation, 'utf-8'); 190: filesModified.push(suggestion.target_location); 191: logger.info({ 192: file: suggestion.target_location, 193: suggestionId: suggestion.suggestion_id 194: }, 'Created consolidated file'); 195: } 196: if (suggestion.migration_steps && suggestion.migration_steps.length > 0) { 197: try { 198: const migrationResult = await this.migrationTransformer.applyMigrationSteps( 199: suggestion, 200: repositoryPath 201: ); 202: for (const file of migrationResult.filesModified) { 203: if (!filesModified.includes(file)) { 204: filesModified.push(file); 205: } 206: } 207: logger.info({ 208: suggestionId: suggestion.suggestion_id, 209: filesModified: migrationResult.filesModified.length, 210: transformations: migrationResult.transformations.length, 211: errors: migrationResult.errors.length, 212: backupPath: migrationResult.backupPath 213: }, 'Applied migration steps'); 214: if (migrationResult.errors.length > 0) { 215: logger.warn({ 216: errors: migrationResult.errors 217: }, 'Some migration transformations failed'); 218: } 219: } catch (migrationError) { 220: logError(logger, migrationError, 'Failed to apply migration steps', { 221: suggestionId: suggestion.suggestion_id 222: }); 223: Sentry.captureException(migrationError, { 224: tags: { 225: component: 'pr-creator', 226: operation: 'apply-migration-steps' 227: }, 228: extra: { 229: suggestionId: suggestion.suggestion_id, 230: repositoryPath 231: } 232: }); 233: } 234: } 235: } catch (error) { 236: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to apply suggestion', { 237: suggestionId: suggestion.suggestion_id 238: }); 239: } 240: } 241: return filesModified; 242: } 243: private async _createPR(cwd: string, branch: string, title: string, body: string): Promise<string> { 244: return runCommand(cwd, 'gh', [ 245: 'pr', 246: 'create', 247: '--title', title, 248: '--body', body, 249: '--base', this.baseBranch, 250: '--head', branch 251: ]); 252: } 253: private _batchSuggestions(suggestions: Suggestion[]): Suggestion[][] { 254: const batches: Suggestion[][] = []; 255: for (let i = 0; i < suggestions.length; i += this.maxSuggestionsPerPR) { 256: batches.push(suggestions.slice(i, i + this.maxSuggestionsPerPR)); 257: } 258: return batches; 259: } 260: private _generateBranchName(_suggestions: Suggestion[], batchNumber: number): string { 261: const timestamp = Date.now(); 262: return `${this.branchPrefix}/batch-${batchNumber}-${timestamp}`; 263: } 264: private _generateCommitMessage(suggestions: Suggestion[], filesModified: string[]): string { 265: const consolidationCount = suggestions.length; 266: const fileCount = filesModified.length; 267: return [ 268: `refactor: consolidate ${consolidationCount} duplicate code pattern${consolidationCount > 1 ? 's' : ''}`, 269: '', 270: `This commit consolidates ${consolidationCount} identified duplicate code pattern${consolidationCount > 1 ? 's' : ''} across ${fileCount} file${fileCount > 1 ? 's' : ''}.`, 271: '', 272: 'Consolidations:', 273: ...suggestions.map((s, i) => `${i + 1}. ${s.target_name || s.suggestion_id}: ${s.strategy_rationale.substring(0, 80)}...`), 274: '', 275: 'Files created:', 276: ...filesModified.map(f => `- ${f}`), 277: '', 278: 'Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>' 279: ].join('\n'); 280: } 281: private _generatePRTitle(suggestions: Suggestion[], batchNumber: number): string { 282: const consolidationCount = suggestions.length; 283: return `refactor: consolidate ${consolidationCount} duplicate code pattern${consolidationCount > 1 ? 's' : ''} (batch ${batchNumber})`; 284: } 285: private _generatePRDescription(suggestions: Suggestion[], filesModified: string[]): string { 286: const consolidationCount = suggestions.length; 287: return [ 288: '## Summary', 289: '', 290: `This PR consolidates ${consolidationCount} identified duplicate code pattern${consolidationCount > 1 ? 's' : ''} to improve code maintainability and reduce duplication.`, 291: '', 292: '## Consolidations', 293: '', 294: ...suggestions.map((s, i) => { 295: return [ 296: `### ${i + 1}. ${s.target_name || s.suggestion_id}`, 297: '', 298: `**Strategy:** ${s.strategy}`, 299: `**Impact Score:** ${s.impact_score}/100`, 300: `**Complexity:** ${s.complexity}`, 301: `**Risk:** ${s.migration_risk}`, 302: '', 303: `**Rationale:** ${s.strategy_rationale}`, 304: '', 305: `**Target Location:** \`${s.target_location}\``, 306: '', 307: s.migration_steps.length > 0 ? '**Migration Steps:**' : '', 308: ...s.migration_steps.map(step => `${step.step_number}. ${step.description}`), 309: '' 310: ].join('\n'); 311: }), 312: '## Files Modified', 313: '', 314: ...filesModified.map(f => `- \`${f}\``), 315: '', 316: '## Testing', 317: '', 318: '- [ ] Unit tests pass', 319: '- [ ] Integration tests pass', 320: '- [ ] Manual testing completed', 321: '- [ ] Code review completed', 322: '', 323: '## Migration Notes', 324: '', 325: 'This PR creates consolidated utility files. The actual migration of existing code to use these utilities should be done in follow-up PRs.', 326: '', 327: suggestions.map((s) => { 328: if (s.usage_example) { 329: return [ 330: `### ${s.target_name || s.suggestion_id} Usage`, 331: '', 332: '```javascript', 333: s.usage_example, 334: '```', 335: '' 336: ].join('\n'); 337: } 338: return ''; 339: }).filter(Boolean).join('\n'), 340: '---', 341: '', 342: 'Generated with [Claude Code](https: 343: ].join('\n'); 344: } 345: } 1: from .code_block import ( 2: CodeBlock, 3: SourceLocation, 4: ASTNode, 5: LanguageType, 6: block_id: str) -> None: ... 48: def set_canonical(self, block_id: str) -> None: ... 49: def __hash__(self) -> int: ... 50: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: ... 1: # Pydantic Models for Duplicate Detection 2: 3: Data models for structuring duplicate detection and consolidation results using Pydantic v2. 4: 5: ## Overview 6: 7: These models define the data structures used throughout the duplicate detection pipeline, from individual code blocks to complete scan reports with consolidation recommendations. 8: 9: ## Model Hierarchy 10: 11: ``` 12: ScanReport (top-level) 13: ├── RepositoryInfo[] # Scanned repositories 14: ├── ScanConfiguration # Scan settings 15: ├── ScanMetrics # Statistical summary 16: ├── code_block_ids[] # → CodeBlock references 17: ├── duplicate_group_ids[] # → DuplicateGroup references 18: └── suggestion_ids[] # → ConsolidationSuggestion references 19: 20: CodeBlock # Individual code pattern 21: ├── SourceLocation # File + line location 22: ├── ASTNode # AST tree structure 23: ├── LanguageType # Programming language enum 24: └── SemanticCategory # Pattern category enum 25: 26: DuplicateGroup # Similar code blocks 27: ├── member_block_ids[] # CodeBlock references 28: ├── SimilarityMethod # How similarity was determined 29: └── computed: impact_score, priority_level 30: 31: ConsolidationSuggestion # Refactoring recommendation 32: ├── ConsolidationStrategy # Tier: local_util → autonomous_agent 33: ├── ImplementationComplexity 34: ├── MigrationRisk 35: ├── MigrationStep[] # Step-by-step guide 36: └── computed: priority, roi_score, is_quick_win 37: ``` 38: 39: ## Models 40: 41: ### CodeBlock 42: 43: Represents a code pattern detected by ast-grep. 44: 45: | Field | Type | Description | 46: |-------|------|-------------| 47: | `block_id` | str | Unique identifier | 48: | `pattern_id` | str | ast-grep rule ID | 49: | `location` | SourceLocation | File path + line numbers | 50: | `source_code` | str | Raw source code | 51: | `language` | LanguageType | javascript, typescript, python, etc. | 52: | `category` | SemanticCategory | utility, api_handler, database_operation, etc. | 53: | `content_hash` | computed | SHA-256 hash for exact matching | 54: | `structural_hash` | computed | AST-based hash for structural matching | 55: 56: ### DuplicateGroup 57: 58: Groups similar CodeBlocks as consolidation candidates. 59: 60: | Field | Type | Description | 61: |-------|------|-------------| 62: | `group_id` | str | Unique identifier | 63: | `member_block_ids` | List[str] | CodeBlock IDs (min 2) | 64: | `similarity_score` | float | 0.0-1.0 similarity | 65: | `similarity_method` | SimilarityMethod | exact_match, structural, semantic, hybrid | 66: | `occurrence_count` | int | Number of instances | 67: | `deduplication_potential` | computed | Lines removable | 68: | `impact_score` | computed | 0-100 consolidation priority | 69: | `priority_level` | computed | critical, high, medium, low | 70: 71: ### ConsolidationSuggestion 72: 73: Actionable recommendation for consolidating duplicates. 74: 75: | Field | Type | Description | 76: |-------|------|-------------| 77: | `suggestion_id` | str | Unique identifier | 78: | `duplicate_group_id` | str | Target DuplicateGroup | 79: | `strategy` | ConsolidationStrategy | local_util, shared_package, mcp_server, autonomous_agent | 80: | `complexity` | ImplementationComplexity | trivial, simple, moderate, complex, very_complex | 81: | `migration_risk` | MigrationRisk | minimal, low, medium, high, critical | 82: | `migration_steps` | List[MigrationStep] | Step-by-step implementation guide | 83: | `priority` | computed | Based on impact + complexity | 84: | `roi_score` | computed | Return on investment (0-100) | 85: | `is_quick_win` | computed | High impact + low effort + low risk | 86: 87: ### ScanReport 88: 89: Top-level report containing all scan results. 90: 91: | Field | Type | Description | 92: |-------|------|-------------| 93: | `report_id` | str | Unique identifier | 94: | `repositories` | List[RepositoryInfo] | Scanned repos | 95: | `configuration` | ScanConfiguration | Rules and thresholds used | 96: | `metrics` | ScanMetrics | Statistical summary | 97: | `duplication_severity` | computed | minimal, low, moderate, high, critical | 98: | `consolidation_opportunity_score` | computed | 0-100 benefit score | 99: 100: ## Enums 101: 102: ### LanguageType 103: ```python 104: JAVASCRIPT, TYPESCRIPT, PYTHON, JAVA, GO, RUST, C, CPP, CSHARP, PHP, RUBY 105: ``` 106: 107: ### SemanticCategory 108: ```python 109: UTILITY, HELPER, VALIDATOR, API_HANDLER, AUTH_CHECK, DATABASE_OPERATION, 110: ERROR_HANDLER, LOGGER, CONFIG_ACCESS, FILE_OPERATION, ASYNC_PATTERN, UNKNOWN 111: ``` 112: 113: ### SimilarityMethod 114: ```python 115: EXACT_MATCH # Identical code 116: STRUCTURAL # Same AST structure 117: SEMANTIC # Similar logic/behavior 118: HYBRID # Combination of methods 119: ``` 120: 121: ### ConsolidationStrategy 122: ```python 123: LOCAL_UTIL # Utility within single project 124: SHARED_PACKAGE # Shared library across 2-3 projects 125: MCP_SERVER # MCP server for cross-language/tool 126: AUTONOMOUS_AGENT # Complex orchestration requiring AI 127: NO_ACTION # Not worth consolidating 128: ``` 129: 130: ## Usage 131: 132: ### Import Models 133: 134: ```python 135: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 136: CodeBlock, 137: DuplicateGroup, 138: ConsolidationSuggestion, 139: ScanReport, 140: LanguageType, 141: SemanticCategory, 142: SimilarityMethod, 143: ) 144: ``` 145: 146: ### Create CodeBlock 147: 148: ```python 149: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 150: CodeBlock, SourceLocation, LanguageType, SemanticCategory 151: ) 152: 153: block = CodeBlock( 154: block_id="cb_001", 155: pattern_id="object-manipulation", 156: location=SourceLocation( 157: file_path="/path/to/repo/src/utils.js", 158: line_start=42, 159: line_end=44 160: ), 161: relative_path="src/utils.js", 162: source_code="JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)", 163: language=LanguageType.JAVASCRIPT, 164: category=SemanticCategory.UTILITY, 165: repository_path="/path/to/repo", 166: line_count=3 167: ) 168: 169: # Access computed hashes 170: print(block.content_hash) # e.g., "a1b2c3d4e5f6..." 171: print(block.structural_hash) # Falls back to content_hash if no AST 172: ``` 173: 174: ### Create DuplicateGroup 175: 176: ```python 177: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import DuplicateGroup, SimilarityMethod 178: 179: group = DuplicateGroup( 180: group_id="dg_001", 181: pattern_id="object-manipulation", 182: member_block_ids=["cb_001", "cb_002", "cb_003"], 183: similarity_score=0.95, 184: similarity_method=SimilarityMethod.STRUCTURAL, 185: category="utility", 186: language="javascript", 187: occurrence_count=3, 188: total_lines=9, 189: affected_files=["src/utils.js", "src/helpers.js"] 190: ) 191: 192: # Access computed fields 193: print(group.deduplication_potential) # Lines removable: 6 194: print(group.impact_score) # 0-100 priority score 195: print(group.priority_level) # "high" 196: print(group.is_cross_repository) # False 197: ``` 198: 199: ### Create ConsolidationSuggestion 200: 201: ```python 202: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 203: ConsolidationSuggestion, 204: ConsolidationStrategy, 205: ImplementationComplexity, 206: MigrationRisk 207: ) 208: 209: suggestion = ConsolidationSuggestion( 210: suggestion_id="cs_001", 211: duplicate_group_id="dg_001", 212: strategy=ConsolidationStrategy.LOCAL_UTIL, 213: strategy_rationale="Used within single project, simple utility function", 214: impact_score=75.0, 215: complexity=ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL, 216: migration_risk=MigrationRisk.LOW, 217: breaking_changes=False, 218: target_location="src/utils/json.js", 219: target_name="writeJsonFile", 220: loc_reduction=15, 221: affected_files_count=5, 222: affected_repositories_count=1, 223: confidence=0.9 224: ) 225: 226: # Access computed fields 227: print(suggestion.priority) # "critical" 228: print(suggestion.roi_score) # Return on investment 229: print(suggestion.is_quick_win) # True 230: 231: # Add migration steps 232: suggestion.add_migration_step( 233: description="Create writeJsonFile function in src/utils/json.js", 234: code_example="export function writeJsonFile(path, data) { ... }", 235: automated=True, 236: estimated_time="15min" 237: ) 238: 239: # Generate markdown summary 240: print(suggestion.to_markdown_summary()) 241: ``` 242: 243: ### Export for Comparison 244: 245: ```python 246: # Export minimal data for clustering algorithms 247: comparison_data = block.to_dict_for_comparison() 248: # Returns: { block_id, pattern_id, category, language, content_hash, ... } 249: 250: # Export summary for dashboards 251: summary = report.to_summary_dict() 252: # Returns: { report_id, duplication_percentage, quick_wins, ... } 253: ``` 254: 255: ## Validation 256: 257: Models include Pydantic validators: 258: 259: ```python 260: # Line range validation 261: SourceLocation(file_path="x.js", line_start=10, line_end=5) 262: # ValidationError: line_end must be >= line_start 263: 264: # Minimum members validation 265: DuplicateGroup(member_block_ids=["cb_001"]) # Only 1 member 266: # ValidationError: A duplicate group must have at least 2 members 267: 268: # Canonical block validation 269: group.set_canonical("cb_999") # Not in member_block_ids 270: # ValueError: Block cb_999 is not a member of this group 271: ``` 272: 273: ## JSON Schema 274: 275: All models support JSON schema export for API documentation: 276: 277: ```python 278: import json 279: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import CodeBlock 280: 281: schema = CodeBlock.model_json_schema() 282: print(json.dumps(schema, indent=2)) 283: ``` 284: 285: ## Related Documentation 286: 287: - [Similarity Algorithm](../../../docs/architecture/similarity-algorithm.md) - How duplicates are detected 288: - [Pipeline Data Flow](../../../docs/architecture/pipeline-data-flow.md) - How models flow through pipeline 289: - [AST-Grep Rules](../../../.ast-grep/README.md) - Pattern detection rules 290: 291: --- 292: 293: 133: occurrence_factor = min(occurrence_count / 20.0, 1.0) 134: similarity_factor = similarity_score 135: loc_factor = min(total_lines / 100.0, 1.0) 136: score = ( 137: occurrence_factor * 40 + 138: similarity_factor * 35 + 139: loc_factor * 25 140: ) 141: return round(score, 2) 142: test_cases = [ 143: (3, 0.95, 9, "Low occurrence, high similarity, small size"), 144: (10, 0.9, 50, "Moderate occurrence, high similarity, moderate size"), 145: (20, 0.8, 100, "High occurrence, good similarity, large size"), 146: ] 147: print("Impact Score Calculation Tests:") 148: for occurrence, similarity, 152: print(f" → Impact Score: {score}/100") 153: def calculate_roi_score(impact_score, estimated_hours): 154: if estimated_hours == 0: 155: return 100.0 156: roi = (impact_score / estimated_hours) * {impact}, Effort: {hours}h") 168: print(f" → ROI Score: {roi}/100") 169: print("-" * 60) 170: def main(): 171: print("\n" + "=" * 60) 172: print("Pydantic Models Structure Test") 173: print("=" * 60) Date.now()); 42: const duration = scanResult.scan_metadata?.duration_seconds ?? 0; 43: return ` 44: <header> 45: <h1>🔍 ${this._escapeHtml(title)}</h1> 46: <div class="header-meta"> 47: <span class="meta-item"> 48: <strong>Scan Type:</strong> ${isInterProject ? 'Inter-Project' : 'Intra-Project'} 49: </span> 50: <span class="meta-item"> 51: <strong>Date:</strong> ${scanDate.toLocaleString()} 52: </span> 53: <span class="meta-item"> 54: <strong>Duration:</strong> ${duration.toFixed(2)}s 55: </span> 56: </div> 57: </header>`; 58: } 59: private static _generateMetrics(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): string { 60: const metrics = scanResult.metrics ?? {}; 61: if (isInterProject) { 62: return ` 63: <section class="metrics"> 64: <h2>📊 Scan Metrics</h2> 65: <div class="metrics-grid"> 66: <div class="metric-card"> 67: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_repositories_scanned ?? 0}</div> 68: <div class="metric-label">Repositories Scanned</div> 69: </div> 70: <div class="metric-card"> 71: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_code_blocks ?? 0}</div> 72: <div class="metric-label">Code Blocks</div> 73: </div> 74: <div class="metric-card highlight"> 75: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_cross_repository_groups ?? 0}</div> 76: <div class="metric-label">Cross-Repo Duplicates</div> 77: </div> 78: <div class="metric-card"> 79: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.cross_repository_duplicated_lines ?? 0}</div> 80: <div class="metric-label">Duplicated Lines</div> 81: </div> 82: <div class="metric-card success"> 83: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.shared_package_candidates ?? 0}</div> 84: <div class="metric-label">Shared Package Candidates</div> 85: </div> 86: <div class="metric-card"> 87: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.mcp_server_candidates ?? 0}</div> 88: <div class="metric-label">MCP Server Candidates</div> 89: </div> 90: </div> 91: </section>`; 92: } 93: return ` 94: <section class="metrics"> 95: <h2>📊 Scan Metrics</h2> 96: <div class="metrics-grid"> 97: <div class="metric-card"> 98: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_code_blocks ?? 0}</div> 99: <div class="metric-label">Code Blocks Detected</div> 100: </div> 101: <div class="metric-card highlight"> 102: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_duplicate_groups ?? 0}</div> 103: <div class="metric-label">Duplicate Groups</div> 104: </div> 105: <div class="metric-card"> 106: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.exact_duplicates ?? (scanResult.cross_repository_suggestions ?? []) 127: : (scanResult.suggestions ?? []); 128: const strategyCounts: Record<string, number> = {}; 129: suggestions.forEach(s => { 130: strategyCounts[s.strategy] = (strategyCounts[s.strategy] ?? 0) + 1; 131: }); 132: const complexityCounts: Record<string, number> = {}; 133: suggestions.forEach(s => { 134: complexityCounts[s.complexity] = (complexityCounts[s.complexity] ?? 0) + 1; 135: }); 136: return ` 137: <section class="charts"> 138: <h2>📈 Distribution</h2> 139: <div class="charts-grid"> 140: <div class="chart-card"> 141: <h3>By Strategy</h3> 142: <div class="chart-bars"> 143: ${Object.entries(strategyCounts).map(([strategy, count]) => ` 144: <div class="chart-bar-row"> 145: <span class="chart-label">${strategy.replace('_', ' ')}</span> 146: <div class="chart-bar-container"> 147: <div class="chart-bar strategy-${strategy}" style="width: ${(count / suggestions.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%"></div> 148: </div> 149: <span class="chart-count">${count}</span> 150: </div> 151: `).join('')} 152: </div> 153: </div> 154: <div class="chart-card"> 155: <h3>By Complexity</h3> 156: <div class="chart-bars"> 157: ${Object.entries(complexityCounts).map(([complexity, count]) => ` 158: <div class="chart-bar-row"> 159: <span class="chart-label">${complexity}</span> 160: <div class="chart-bar-container"> 161: <div class="chart-bar complexity-${complexity}" style="width: ${(count / suggestions.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%"></div> 162: </div> 163: <span class="chart-count">${count}</span> 164: </div> 165: `).join('')} 166: </div> 167: </div> 168: </div> 169: </section>`; 170: } 171: private static _generateCrossRepoSection(scanResult: ScanResult): string { 172: const repos = scanResult.scanned_repositories ?? []; 173: return ` 174: <section class="cross-repo"> 175: <h2>🔗 Scanned Repositories</h2> 176: <div class="repo-grid"> 177: ${repos.map(repo => ` 178: <div class="repo-card ${repo.error ? 'error' : ''}"> 179: <div class="repo-name">${this._escapeHtml(repo.name)}</div> 180: ${repo.error ? ` 181: <div class="repo-error">❌ ${this._escapeHtml(repo.error)}</div> 182: ` : ` 183: <div class="repo-stats"> 184: <span>${repo.code_blocks ?? 0} blocks</span> 185: <span>${repo.duplicate_groups ?? 0} groups</span> 186: </div> 187: `} 188: </div> 189: `).join('')} 190: </div> 191: </section>`; 192: } 193: private static _generateDuplicateGroups(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): string { 194: const groups = isInterProject 195: ? (scanResult.cross_repository_duplicates ?? []) 196: : (scanResult.duplicate_groups ?? []); 197: const topGroups = groups 198: .sort((a, b) => b.impact_score - a.impact_score) 199: .slice(0, 10); 200: if (topGroups.length === 0) { 201: return ` 202: <section class="duplicates"> 203: <h2>🎯 Duplicate Groups</h2> 204: <p class="empty-state">No duplicate groups detected.</p> 205: </section>`; 206: } 207: return ` 208: <section class="duplicates"> 209: <h2>🎯 Top Duplicate Groups</h2> 210: <div class="duplicates-list"> 211: ${topGroups.map((group, index) => ` 212: <div class="duplicate-card"> 213: <div class="duplicate-header"> 214: <div class="duplicate-rank">#${index + 1}</div> 215: <div class="duplicate-title"> 216: <strong>${this._escapeHtml(group.group_id)}</strong> 217: <span class="duplicate-pattern">${this._escapeHtml(group.pattern_id)}</span> 218: </div> 219: <div class="duplicate-impact ${this._getImpactClass(group.impact_score)}"> 220: ${group.impact_score.toFixed(0)}% impact 221: </div> 222: </div> 223: <div class="duplicate-stats"> 224: <span class="stat-badge"> 225: 📦 ${group.occurrence_count} occurrences 226: </span> 227: ${isInterProject ? ` 228: <span class="stat-badge"> 229: 🔗 ${group.repository_count ?? 0} repositories 230: </span> 231: ` : ''} 232: <span class="stat-badge"> 233: 📄 ${group.affected_files?.length ?? 0} files 234: </span> 235: <span class="stat-badge"> 236: 📏 ${group.total_lines} lines 237: </span> 238: </div> 239: <div class="duplicate-files"> 240: <strong>Affected files:</strong> 241: <ul> 242: ${(group.affected_files ?? '')}</p> 291: ${suggestion.target_location ? ` 292: <p class="suggestion-target"> 293: <strong>Target:</strong> <code>${this._escapeHtml(suggestion.target_location)}</code> 294: </p> 295: ` : ''} 296: <div class="suggestion-metrics"> 297: <span class="metric-badge"> 298: Impact: ${suggestion.impact_score.toFixed(0)}% 299: </span> 300: <span class="metric-badge complexity-${suggestion.complexity}"> 301: ${suggestion.complexity} complexity 302: </span> 303: <span class="metric-badge risk-${suggestion.migration_risk}"> 304: ${suggestion.migration_risk} risk 305: </span> 306: ${suggestion.estimated_effort_hours != null ? ` 307: <span class="metric-badge"> 308: ~${suggestion.estimated_effort_hours}h effort 309: </span> 310: ` : ''} 311: </div> 312: ${suggestion.breaking_changes ? ` 313: <div class="warning-box"> 314: ⚠️ <strong>Breaking Change:</strong> This consolidation may require API changes 315: </div> 316: ` : ''} 317: </div> 318: </div> 319: `).join('')} 320: </div> 321: </section>`; 83: if (outputFile) { 84: await fs.writeFile(outputFile, report); 85: logger.info(`Markdown report saved to: ${outputFile}`); 86: } else { 87: console.log('\n' + report); 88: } 89: } 90: logger.info('Scan complete!'); 91: process.exit(0); 92: } catch (error) { 93: logError(logger, error, 'Scan failed'); 94: process.exit(1); 95: } 96: } 97: function generateMarkdownReport(results: ScanResults): string { 98: const lines: string[] = [ 99: '# Codebase Health Report', 100: '', 101: `**Repository:** ${results.repository}`, 102: `**Generated:** ${results.timestamp}`, 103: '', 104: '---', 105: '' 106: ]; 107: // Timeout scan report 108: if (results.scans.timeout) { 109: const timeout = results.scans.timeout; 110: const detector = new TimeoutPatternDetector({ logger }); 111: lines.push('## Timeout Pattern Detection'); 112: lines.push(''); 113: lines.push(detector.generateReport(timeout)); 114: lines.push(''); 115: } 116: // Root directory analysis report 117: if (results.scans.root) { 118: const root = results.scans.root; 119: const analyzer = new RootDirectoryAnalyzer({ logger }); 120: lines.push('## Root Directory Analysis'); 121: lines.push(''); 122: lines.push(analyzer.generateReport(root)); 123: lines.push(''); 124: } 125: // Summary 126: lines.push('---', '', '## Summary', ''); 127: if (results.scans.timeout) { 128: const stats = results.scans.timeout.statistics; 129: lines.push( 130: `- **Timeout Issues:** ${stats.total_issues} issues in ${stats.affected_files} files`, 131: ` - High: ${stats.severity_breakdown.high || 0}`, 132: ` - Medium: ${stats.severity_breakdown.medium || 0}`, 133: ` - Low: ${stats.severity_breakdown.low || 0}`, 134: '' 135: ); 136: } 137: if (results.scans.root) { 138: const stats = results.scans.root.statistics; 139: lines.push( 140: `- **Root Directory:** ${stats.total_root_files} files (${stats.reduction_percentage}% reduction possible)`, 141: ` - Can move: ${stats.reduction_potential} files`, 142: ` - Final count: ${stats.final_root_files} files`, 143: '' 144: ); 145: } 146: lines.push( 147: '---', 148: '', 149: '## Next Steps', 150: '', 151: '1. Implement fixes incrementally', 155: '5. For comprehensive scanning, use the JavaScript version with ast-grep. 319: 320: --- 321: 322: ## Configuration 323: 324: ### Customize Thresholds 325: 326: Edit scanner files to adjust sensitivity: 327: 328: **Root Directory Analyzer:** 329: ```javascript 330: // lib/scanners/root-directory-analyzer.js 331: this.maxRootFiles = 20; // Change to your preference 332: this.thresholds = { 333: pythonFiles: 3, // Warn if > 3 Python files in root 334: shellScripts: 3, 335: jsFiles: 3, 336: configFiles: 5, 337: dataFiles: 0 // Never allow data files in root 338: }; 339: ``` 340: 341: **Timeout Pattern Detector:** 342: ```javascript 343: // lib/scanners/timeout-pattern-detector.js 344: // Add custom patterns in scan() method 345: const customIssues = await this.findCustomPattern(repoPath); 346: ``` 347: 348: --- 349: 350: ## Real Results 351: 352: ### AnalyticsBot Dashboard (Nov 18, 2025) 353: 354: **Problem:** 355: - Dashboard spinning forever 356: - No error messages 357: - Required browser restart 358: 359: **Scan Results:** 360: ``` 361: 🔴 HIGH: src/api/client.ts:261 362: Promise.race() in getAnalyticsSummary() without timeout 363: 364: 🔴 HIGH: src/api/client.ts:469 365: Promise.race() in listProjects() without timeout 366: 367: 🟡 MEDIUM: src/hooks/useDashboardData.ts:46 368: setLoading(true) without finally block 369: ``` 370: 371: **Fixes Applied:** 372: 1. 44: } 45: export class RepositoryScanner { 46: private readonly repomixWorker: InstanceType<typeof RepomixWorker>; 47: constructor(options: RepositoryScannerOptions = {}) { 48: this.repomixWorker = new RepomixWorker({ 49: outputBaseDir: options.outputBaseDir ?? config.outputBaseDir, 50: codeBaseDir: options.codeBaseDir ?? config.codeBaseDir, 51: maxConcurrent: options.maxConcurrent ?? config.maxConcurrent 52: }); 53: } 54: async scanRepository(repoPath: string, scanConfig: ScanConfig = {}): Promise<RepositoryScanOutput> { 55: const startTime = Date.now(); 56: logStart(logger, 'repository scan', { repoPath }); 57: try { 58: await this.validateRepository(repoPath); 59: const repoInfo = await this.getRepositoryInfo(repoPath); 60: let metadata: RepomixMetadata = { totalFiles: 0, totalLines: 0, languages: [] }; 61: let repomixOutput: string | null = null; 62: try { 63: const repomixResult = await this.runRepomixScan(repoPath); 64: metadata = await this.parseRepomixOutput(repomixResult.outputFile as string); 65: repomixOutput = repomixResult.outputFile as string; 66: } catch (error) { 67: logger.warn({ error: (error as Error).message }, 'Repomix scan failed, using basic file discovery'); 68: const files = await this.listFiles(repoPath); 69: metadata = { 70: totalFiles: files.length, 71: totalLines: 0, 72: languages: this.detectLanguages(files) 73: }; 74: } 75: const fileMetadata = await this.getFileMetadata(repoPath, scanConfig); 76: const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000; 77: logger.info({ 78: repoPath, 79: totalFiles: metadata.totalFiles, 80: duration 81: }, 'Repository scan completed'); 82: return { 83: repository_info: { 84: ...repoInfo, 85: total_files: metadata.totalFiles, 86: total_lines: metadata.totalLines, 87: languages: metadata.languages 88: }, 89: file_metadata: fileMetadata, 90: scan_metadata: { 91: duration_seconds: duration, 92: repomix_output: repomixOutput, 93: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 94: used_repomix: repomixOutput !== null 95: } 96: }; 97: } catch (error) { 98: logger.error({ repoPath, error }, 'Repository scan failed'); 99: throw new RepositoryScanError(`Failed to scan repository: ${(error as Error).message}`, { 100: cause: error 101: }); 102: } 103: } 104: async validateRepository(repoPath: string): Promise<void> { 105: try { 106: const stats = await fs.stat(repoPath); 107: if (!stats.isDirectory()) { 108: throw new Error('Path is not a directory'); 109: } 110: try { 111: await fs.access(path.join(repoPath, '.git')); 112: } catch { 113: logger.warn({ repoPath }, 'Not a git repository, proceeding anyway'); 114: } 115: } catch (error) { 116: throw new Error(`Invalid repository path: ${(error as Error).message}`);
