128: try { 129: const gitDir = path.join(repoPath, '.git'); 130: await fs.access(gitDir); 131: try { 132: info.git_remote = execSync('git config --get remote.origin.url', { 133: cwd: repoPath, 134: encoding: 'utf-8' 135: }).trim(); 136: } catch { 137: logger.debug({ repoPath }, 'No git remote found'); 138: } 139: try { 140: info.git_branch = execSync('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', { 141: cwd: repoPath, 142: encoding: 'utf-8' 143: }).trim(); 162: const job = this.repomixWorker.createRepomixJob(repoPath, relativePath); 163: return await this.repomixWorker.runJobHandler(job) as Record<string, unknown>; 164: } 165: async parseRepomixOutput(outputFile: string): Promise<RepomixMetadata> { 166: try { 167: const content = await fs.readFile(outputFile, 'utf-8'); 174: if (dirStructMatch) { 175: const structure = dirStructMatch[1]; 176: metadata.totalFiles = (structure.match(/\n\s+[^\s/]+\.[^\s/]+$/gm) ?? []).length; 177: } 178: const fileMatches = content.matchAll(/<file path="([^"]+)">/g); 179: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 180: '.js': 'javascript', 181: '.ts': 'typescript', 182: '.jsx': 'javascript', 183: '.tsx': 'typescript', 184: '.py': 'python', 185: '.go': 'go', 186: '.rs': 'rust', 187: '.java': 'java', 188: '.rb': 'ruby', 189: '.php': 'php' 190: }; 191: for (const match of fileMatches) { 192: metadata.totalFiles++; 193: const ext = path.extname(match[1]).toLowerCase(); 194: if (langMap[ext]) { 195: metadata.languages.add(langMap[ext]); 196: } 197: } 198: const linesMatch = content.match(/\n/g); 199: metadata.totalLines = linesMatch ? linesMatch.length : 0; 200: return { 201: totalFiles: metadata.totalFiles, 202: totalLines: metadata.totalLines, 203: languages: Array.from(metadata.languages) 204: }; 205: } catch (error) { 206: logger.warn({ outputFile, error }, 'Failed to parse repomix output'); 207: return { 208: totalFiles: 0, 209: totalLines: 0, 210: languages: [] 211: }; 212: } 213: } 214: async getFileMetadata(_repoPath: string, _scanConfig: ScanConfig): Promise<unknown[]> { 215: return []; 220: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 221: '.js': 'javascript', 222: '.ts': 'typescript', 223: '.jsx': 'javascript', 224: '.tsx': 'typescript', 225: '.py': 'python' 226: }; 227: async function walk(dir: string): Promise<void> { 228: const entries = await fs.readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true }); 229: for (const entry of entries) { 230: const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name); 231: if (entry.isDirectory()) { 232: if (ignoredDirs.has(entry.name)) { 233: continue; 234: } 235: await walk(fullPath); 236: } else { 237: if (filter.languages) { 238: const ext = path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase(); 239: if (filter.languages.includes(langMap[ext])) { 240: files.push(fullPath); 241: } 242: } else { 243: files.push(fullPath); 244: } 245: } 246: } 247: } 248: await walk(repoPath); 249: return files; 250: } 251: detectLanguages(files: string[]): string[] { 252: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 253: '.js': 'javascript', 254: '.ts': 'typescript', 255: '.jsx': 'javascript', 256: '.tsx': 'typescript', 257: '.py': 'python', 258: '.go': 'go', 259: '.rs': 'rust', 260: '.java': 'java', 261: '.rb': 'ruby', 262: '.php': 'php' 263: }; 403: `- **Severity:** ${item.severity}`, 404: `- **Message:** ${item.message}`, 405: `- **Recommendation:** ${item.recommendation}`, 406: '' 407: ); 408: if (item.fix_example) { 409: lines.push('```typescript', item.fix_example.trim(), '```', ''); 410: } 411: }); 412: } 413: } 414: return lines.join('\n'); 415: } 416: } 417: export function createTimeoutPatternDetector(options?: 105: } else if (cacheAge > this.warningThreshold) { 106: logger.warn({ 107: cacheAgeHours, 108: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime), 109: newestFile 110: }, 'Doppler cache is aging - approaching staleness threshold'); 111: Sentry.captureMessage('Doppler cache aging', { 112: level: 'warning', 113: extra: { 114: cacheAgeHours, 115: cacheAgeMinutes, 116: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime).toISOString(), 117: threshold: this.warningThreshold / TIME.HOUR, 118: newestFile, 119: fileCount: secretFiles.length 120: }, 121: tags: { 122: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 123: severity: 'warning' 124: } 125: }); 126: } else { 127: logger.debug({ 128: cacheAgeHours, 129: cacheAgeMinutes, 130: fileCount: secretFiles.length 131: }, 'Doppler cache health check - within thresholds'); 132: } 133: return status; 134: } catch (error) { 135: const nodeError = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException; 148: } 149: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to check Doppler cache health', { cacheDir: this.cacheDir }); 150: Sentry.captureException(error, { 151: tags: { 152: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 153: operation: 'check-cache-health' 154: } 155: }); repository_path: string }> = []; 545: for (const repoScan of interProjectResult.repository_scans) { 546: if (repoScan.error) { 547: results.push({ 548: error: repoScan.error, 549: repository_path: repoScan.repository_path 550: }); 551: } else if (repoScan.scan_result) { 552: results.push(repoScan.scan_result); 553: } 554: } 555: return { 556: repositories: results, 557: total_scanned: repoPaths.length, 558: successful: results.filter(r => !('error' in r)).length, 559: failed: results.filter(r => 'error' in r).length, 560: cross_repository_duplicates: interProjectResult.cross_repository_duplicates, 561: cross_repository_suggestions: interProjectResult.cross_repository_suggestions, 562: scan_type: 'inter-project', 563: metrics: interProjectResult.metrics 564: }; 565: } catch (error) { 566: logger.error({ error }, 'Multi-repository scan failed'); 567: logger.warn('Falling back to individual repository scans without cross-repository analysis'); 568: const results: Array<ScanResult | { error: string; repository_path: string }> = []; 569: for (const repoPath of repoPaths) { 570: try { 571: const result = await this.scanRepository(repoPath, scanConfig); 572: results.push(result); 573: } catch (scanError) { 574: logger.warn({ repoPath, error: scanError }, 'Repository scan failed, continuing'); 575: results.push({ 576: error: (scanError as Error).message, 577: repository_path: repoPath 578: }); 579: } 580: } 581: return { 582: repositories: results, 583: total_scanned: repoPaths.length, 584: successful: results.filter(r => !('error' in r)).length, 585: failed: results.filter(r => 'error' in r).length, 586: scan_type: 'single-project' 587: }; 588: } 589: } 590: } 591: export class ScanError extends Error { 592: constructor(message: string, options?: { cause?: unknown }) { 593: super(message); 594: if (options?.cause) { 595: (this as unknown as Record<string, unknown>).cause = options.cause; 596: } 597: this.name = 'ScanError'; 598: } 599: } 1: # Universal Repository Cleanup Script 2: 3: **Location:** `~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh` 4: **Created:** 2025-11-17 5: **Purpose:** Clean up common bloat from any repository 6: 7: ## Quick Start 8: 9: ```bash 10: # Clean current directory 11: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 12: 13: # Clean specific directory 14: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /path/to/repo 15: 16: # Clean a project 17: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/myapp 18: ``` 19: 20: ## What It Does 21: 22: This script automatically detects and removes common bloat from repositories: 23: 24: ### 1. Python Virtual Environments 25: Removes directories matching common venv patterns: 26: - `venv/`, `.venv/`, `env/`, `.env/` 27: - `virtualenv/`, `*.venv/` 28: - Custom patterns (e.g., `personal_site/`) 29: 30: ### 2. Temporary/Cache Files 31: Removes system and editor temporary files: 32: - `.DS_Store` (macOS) 33: - `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `*.pyo` (Python) 34: - `*.swp`, `*.swo`, `*~` (Vim/editor) 35: - `Thumbs.db`, `desktop.ini` (Windows) 36: 37: ### 3. Output/Generated Files 38: Removes generated output files: 39: - `repomix-output.xml` (nested, keeps root) 40: - `*.log`, `npm-debug.log*` 41: - `yarn-debug.log*`, `yarn-error.log*` 42: 43: ### 4. Build Artifacts 44: Removes common build directories: 45: - `.jekyll-cache`, `.sass-cache`, `.bundle` (Jekyll) 46: - `dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `.output/` (Build outputs) 47: - `.next/`, `.nuxt/` (Framework caches) 48: - `node_modules/.cache/` (NPM cache) 49: - `target/`, `.gradle/` (Java/Gradle) 50: 51: ### 5. Redundant Directories 52: Removes common redundant directories: 53: - `drafts/`, `temp/`, `tmp/` 54: - `backup/`, `backups/`, `old/` 55: - `archive/`, `deprecated/` 56: 57: ## Features 58: 59: ✅ **Safe & Interactive** 60: - Preview what will be removed before deletion 61: - Shows sizes and counts 62: - Requires explicit "yes" confirmation 63: 64: ✅ **Intelligent Scanning** 65: - Skips `node_modules/` when looking for venvs 66: - Preserves root-level `repomix-output.xml` 67: - Detects `.gitignore` entries 68: 69: ✅ **Universal Compatibility** 70: - Works on any repository type 71: - Configurable patterns 72: - Language agnostic 73: 74: ✅ **Visual Feedback** 75: - Color-coded output (green/yellow/red/blue) 76: - Progress indicators (✓ ✗ ⚠ →) 77: - Size calculations for removed items 78: 79: ## Usage Examples 80: 81: ### Example 1: Personal Website 82: ```bash 83: cd ~/projects/PersonalSite 84: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 85: ``` 86: 87: **Output:** 88: ``` 89: ======================================== 90: Repository Cleanup Preview 91: ======================================== 92: 93: Target Directory: /Users/you/projects/PersonalSite 94: Current Size: 616M 95: 96: Python Virtual Environments (2 found): 97: - personal_site (40M) 98: - utils/numpy (42M) 99: 100: Temporary/Cache Files (3 found): 101: - 3 files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, .swp, etc.) 102: 103: Build Artifacts (2 found): 104: - .jekyll-cache (5M) 105: - dist (123K) 106: 107: Total items to remove: 7 108: 109: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): 110: ``` 111: 112: ### Example 2: React Project 113: ```bash 114: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/my-react-app 115: ``` 116: 117: Cleans: 118: - `.next/` cache 119: - `dist/` build output 120: - `.DS_Store` files 121: - `npm-debug.log` files 122: 123: ### Example 3: Python Project 124: ```bash 125: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/django-app 126: ``` 127: 128: Cleans: 129: - `venv/`, `.venv/` directories 130: - `__pycache__/` directories 131: - `*.pyc` files 132: - `*.log` files 133: 134: ## Customization 135: 136: Edit the script to customize what gets cleaned: 137: 138: ```bash 139: # Add custom venv patterns 140: VENV_PATTERNS=( 141: "venv" 142: ".venv" 143: "your_custom_venv_name" # Add here 144: ) 145: 146: # Add custom build artifacts 147: BUILD_ARTIFACTS=( 148: "dist" 149: "build" 150: "your_build_dir" # Add here 151: ) 152: 153: # Add custom redundant directories 154: REDUNDANT_DIRS=( 155: "drafts" 156: "temp" 157: "your_backup_dir" # Add here 158: ) 159: ``` 160: 161: ## Safety Features 162: 163: ### Preview Before Deletion 164: Always shows what will be removed with sizes before asking for confirmation 165: 166: ### No Accidental Deletions 167: - Requires explicit "yes" to proceed 168: - Exit codes on errors (`set -e`) 169: - Validates directory exists 170: 171: ### Preserves Important Files 172: - Skips `node_modules/` when scanning 173: - Keeps root-level `repomix-output.xml` 174: - Respects `.gitignore` patterns 175: 176: ### Error Handling 177: - Validates target directory exists 178: - Handles missing files gracefully 179: - Reports errors clearly 180: 181: ## Output Format 182: 183: ### Summary Report 184: ``` 185: ======================================== 186: Cleanup Summary 187: ======================================== 188: 189: Cleanup completed successfully! 190: 191: Target Directory: /Users/you/projects/repo 192: Final Size: 150M 193: 194: Cleaned up: 195: ✓ Python virtual environments 196: ✓ Temporary/cache files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, etc.) 197: ✓ Output/generated files (logs, repomix files, etc.) 198: ✓ Build artifacts (.jekyll-cache, dist/, etc.) 199: ✓ Redundant directories (drafts/, temp/, backup/, etc.) 200: ``` 201: 202: ### Recommendations 203: ``` 204: ======================================== 205: Recommendations 206: ======================================== 207: 208: Consider adding these patterns to .gitignore if not already present: 209: 210: # Python 211: venv/ 212: .venv/ 213: *.pyc 214: __pycache__/ 215: 216: # System files 217: .DS_Store 218: Thumbs.db 219: 220: # Build artifacts 221: dist/ 222: build/ 223: *.log 224: ``` 225: 226: ## Common Use Cases 227: 228: ### 1. Before Committing 229: Clean up before committing to ensure no build artifacts or venvs are added: 230: ```bash 231: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 232: git status 233: ``` 234: 235: ### 2. After Cloning 236: Clean up a cloned repo that has committed bloat: 237: ```bash 238: git clone https://github.com/user/repo 239: cd repo 240: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 241: ``` 242: 243: ### 3. Periodic Maintenance 244: Run monthly to keep repos clean: 245: ```bash 246: # Add to cron or run manually 247: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/myapp 248: ``` 249: 250: ### 4. Disk Space Recovery 251: Find and clean the biggest repos: 252: ```bash 253: # Find large directories 254: du -sh ~/projects/* | sort -hr | head -10 255: 256: # Clean the largest 257: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/largest-repo 258: ``` 259: 260: ## Integration with Git 261: 262: ### Pre-commit Hook 263: Add to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`: 264: ```bash 265: #!/bin/bash 266: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" 267: ``` 268: 269: ### Alias 270: Add to `~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`: 271: ```bash 272: alias cleanup='~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh' 273: 274: # Usage: 275: # cd my-repo 276: # cleanup 277: ``` 278: 279: ## Comparison with Original Script 280: 281: | Feature | Original (PersonalSite) | Universal | 282: |---------|------------------------|-----------| 283: | Target | Single repo | Any directory | 284: | Patterns | Jekyll-specific | Universal | 285: | Usage | `./utils/cleanup-repository.sh` | `script.sh [directory]` | 286: | Customization | Hardcoded | Configurable arrays | 287: | Scanning | Manual checks | Automatic detection | 288: | Portability | Project-specific | Portable | 289: 290: ## Troubleshooting 291: 292: ### "Directory does not exist" 293: ```bash 294: # Check the path 295: ls -la /path/to/repo 296: 297: # Use absolute path 298: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /full/path/to/repo 299: ``` 300: 301: ### "No items found to clean" 302: Repository is already clean! This is normal if: 303: - Already ran the script 304: - Repo has good .gitignore 305: - No bloat present 306: 307: ### Script shows items but doesn't remove 308: Make sure you typed "yes" exactly (case-sensitive): 309: ``` 310: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): yes 311: ``` 312: 313: ### Want to skip confirmation (dangerous) 314: ```bash 315: # Auto-confirm (use with caution!) 316: echo "yes" | ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /path/to/repo 317: ``` 318: 319: ## Advanced Usage 320: 321: ### Dry Run (Preview Only) 322: The script always shows preview first - just answer "no" to confirmation: 323: ``` 324: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): no 325: ``` 326: 327: ### Clean Multiple Repos 328: ```bash 329: #!/bin/bash 330: for repo in ~/projects/*; do 331: if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then 332: echo "Cleaning $repo..." 333: echo "yes" | ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh "$repo" 334: fi 335: done 336: ``` 337: 338: ### Selective Cleanup 339: Comment out functions in `main()` to skip categories: 340: ```bash 341: main() { 342: # ... 343: cleanup_venvs 344: cleanup_temp_files 345: # cleanup_output_files # Skip this 346: # cleanup_build_artifacts # Skip this 347: cleanup_redundant_dirs 348: # ... 349: } 350: ``` 351: 352: ## Performance 353: 354: ### Speed 355: - Small repos (<100MB): ~1-2 seconds 356: - Medium repos (500MB): ~5-10 seconds 357: - Large repos (1GB+): ~30-60 seconds 358: 359: ### Resource Usage 360: - Memory: Minimal (<10MB) 361: - CPU: Low (scanning files) 362: - Disk: Frees up space 363: 364: ## Best Practices 365: 366: 1. **Run before committing** - Clean before adding files to git 367: 2. **Review preview** - Always check what will be removed 368: 3. **Update .gitignore** - Add patterns to prevent reintroduction 369: 4. **Backup first** - For unfamiliar repos, backup before running 370: 5. **Test builds** - After cleanup, test that builds still work 371: 372: ## Version History 373: 374: | Version | Date | Changes | 375: |---------|------|---------| 376: | 1.0.0 | 2025-11-17 | Initial universal version from PersonalSite cleanup script | 377: 378: ## Related Scripts 379: 380: - **Original:** `/Users/alyshialedlie/code/PersonalSite/utils/cleanup-repository.sh` 381: - PersonalSite-specific version 382: - More detailed verification 383: - Jekyll-focused 384: 385: - **Universal:** `~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh` 386: - Works on any repo 387: - Configurable patterns 388: - Portable 389: 390: ## Support 391: 392: For issues or improvements: 393: 1. Review this documentation 394: 2. Check script comments for customization 395: 3. Edit pattern arrays for specific needs 396: 4. 2: import { SidequestServer } from '../core/server.ts'; 3: import { logError } from '../utils/logger.ts'; 4: import cron from 'node-cron'; 5: import type { Logger } from 'pino'; 6: export type { Job, JobStats }; 7: export abstract class BasePipeline<TWorker extends SidequestServer = SidequestServer> { 8: protected worker: TWorker; 9: constructor(worker: TWorker) { 10: this.worker = worker; 11: } 12: waitForCompletion(): Promise<void> { 13: return new Promise<void>((resolve) => { 14: const cleanup = () => { 15: this.worker.off('job:completed', checkAndResolve); 16: this.worker.off('job:failed', checkAndResolve); 17: this.worker.off('retry:created', checkAndResolve); 18: }; 19: const checkAndResolve = () => { 20: const stats = this.worker.getStats(); 21: if (stats.active === 0 && stats.queued === 0) { 22: cleanup(); 23: resolve(); 24: } 25: }; 26: this.worker.on('job:completed', checkAndResolve); 27: this.worker.on('job:failed', checkAndResolve); 28: this.worker.on('retry:created', checkAndResolve); 29: checkAndResolve(); 30: }); 31: } 32: protected scheduleCron( 33: logger: Logger, 34: name: string, 35: cronSchedule: string, 36: runFn: () => Promise<unknown> 37: ): cron.ScheduledTask { 38: if (!cron.validate(cronSchedule)) { 39: throw new Error(`Invalid cron schedule: ${cronSchedule}`); 40: } 41: logger.info({ cronSchedule }, `Scheduling ${name}`); 42: const task = cron.schedule(cronSchedule, async () => { 43: logger.info(`Cron triggered - starting ${name}`); 44: try { 45: await runFn(); 46: } catch (error) { 47: logError(logger, error, `Scheduled ${name} failed`); 48: } 49: }); 50: logger.info(`${name} scheduled`); 51: return task; 52: } 53: getStats(): JobStats { 54: return this.worker.getStats(); 55: } 56: } 1: #!/usr/bin/env -S node --strip-types 2: import { BugfixAuditWorker } from '../workers/bugfix-audit-worker.ts'; 3: import { config } from '../core/config.ts'; 4: import { createComponentLogger, logError, logStart } from '../utils/logger.ts'; 5: import { BasePipeline, type Job, type JobStats } from './base-pipeline.ts'; 6: import fs from 'fs/promises'; 7: import path from 'path'; 8: const logger = createComponentLogger('BugfixAuditPipeline'); 9: interface BugfixAuditOptions { 10: maxConcurrent?: number; 11: activeDocsDir?: string; 12: outputBaseDir?: string; 13: gitBaseBranch?: string; 14: gitBranchPrefix?: string; 15: gitDryRun?: boolean; 16: [key: string]: unknown; 17: } 18: interface JobData { 19: projectName: string; 20: markdownFile: string; 21: repoPath: string; 22: } 23: interface JobResult { 24: pullRequestUrl?: string; 25: } 26: interface RunResult { 27: status: string; 28: jobsCreated?: number; 29: duration?: number; 30: active?: number; 31: queued?: number; 32: total?: number; 33: completed?: number; 34: failed?: number; 35: } 36: class BugfixAuditPipeline extends BasePipeline<BugfixAuditWorker> { 37: constructor(options: BugfixAuditOptions = {}) { 38: super(new BugfixAuditWorker({ 39: ...options, 40: maxConcurrent: options.maxConcurrent ?? 3, 41: logDir: config.logDir, 42: sentryDsn: config.sentryDsn, 43: activeDocsDir: options.activeDocsDir ?? path.join(config.homeDir, 'dev', 'active'), 44: outputBaseDir: options.outputBaseDir ?? path.join(config.homeDir, 'code', 'jobs', 'sidequest', 'bug-fixes', 'output'), 45: gitBaseBranch: options.gitBaseBranch ?? config.gitBaseBranch, 46: gitBranchPrefix: options.gitBranchPrefix ?? 'bugfix', 47: gitDryRun: options.gitDryRun ?? config.gitDryRun, 48: })); 49: this.setupEventListeners(); 50: } 51: private setupEventListeners(): void { 52: this.worker.on('job:created', (job: Job) => { 53: const data = job.data as unknown as JobData; 54: logger.info({ 55: jobId: job.id, 56: projectName: data.projectName, 57: markdownFile: data.markdownFile, 58: }, 'Bugfix audit job created'); 59: }); 60: this.worker.on('job:started', (job: Job) => { 61: const data = job.data as unknown as JobData; 62: logger.info({ 63: jobId: job.id, 64: projectName: data.projectName, 65: }, 'Bugfix audit job started'); 66: }); 67: this.worker.on('job:completed', (job: Job) => { 68: const data = job.data as unknown as JobData; 69: const result = job.result as unknown as JobResult | undefined; 70: const duration = job.completedAt && job.startedAt 71: ? job.completedAt.getTime() - job.startedAt.getTime() 72: : undefined; 73: logger.info({ 74: jobId: job.id, 75: projectName: data.projectName, 76: pullRequestUrl: result?.pullRequestUrl, 77: duration, 78: }, 'Bugfix audit job completed'); 79: }); 80: this.worker.on('job:failed', (job: Job) => { 81: const data = job.data as unknown as JobData; 82: logError(logger, job.error, 'Bugfix audit job failed', { 83: jobId: job.id, 84: projectName: data.projectName, 85: }); 86: }); 87: } 88: async runBugfixAudit(): Promise<RunResult> { 89: logStart(logger, 'bugfix audit pipeline', { timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }); 90: const startTime = Date.now(); 91: const jobs = await this.worker.createJobsForAllMarkdownFiles(); 92: if (jobs.length === 0) { 93: logger.info('No eligible projects found for bug fix audit'); 94: return { status: 'completed', jobsCreated: 0 }; 95: } 96: logger.info({ jobCount: jobs.length }, 'Jobs created, waiting for completion'); 97: await this.waitForCompletion(); 98: const duration = Date.now() - startTime; 99: const stats = this.getStats(); 100: logger.info({ 101: durationSeconds: Math.round(duration / 1000), 102: totalJobs: stats.total, 103: completed: stats.completed, 104: failed: stats.failed, 105: }, 'Bugfix audit complete'); 106: await this.saveRunSummary(stats, duration, jobs); 107: return { status: 'completed', duration, ...stats }; 108: } 109: private async saveRunSummary(stats: JobStats, duration: number, jobs: Job[]): Promise<void> { 110: const summary = { 111: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 112: duration, 113: stats, 114: jobs: jobs.map((job) => { 115: const data = job.data as unknown as JobData; 116: const result = job.result as unknown as JobResult | undefined; 117: return { 118: id: job.id, 119: projectName: data.projectName, 120: markdownFile: data.markdownFile, 121: repoPath: data.repoPath, 122: status: job.status, 123: pullRequestUrl: result?.pullRequestUrl, 124: }; 125: }), 126: }; 127: const logsDir = path.join(config.homeDir, 'code', 'jobs', 'sidequest', 'bug-fixes', 'logs'); 128: await fs.mkdir(logsDir, { recursive: true }); 129: const summaryPath = path.join(logsDir, `run-summary-${Date.now()}.json`); 130: await fs.writeFile(summaryPath, JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)); 131: logger.info({ summaryPath }, 'Run summary saved'); 132: } 133: scheduleAudits(cronSchedule: string = '0 1 * * *') { 134: return this.scheduleCron(logger, 'bugfix audit', cronSchedule, () => this.runBugfixAudit()); 135: } 136: scheduleTonight(): void { 137: const now = new Date(); 138: const tonight1AM = new Date(); 139: tonight1AM.setHours(1, 0, 0, 0); 140: if (now.getHours() >= 1) { 141: tonight1AM.setDate(tonight1AM.getDate() + 1); 142: } 143: const delay = tonight1AM.getTime() - now.getTime(); 144: logger.info({ 145: scheduledTime: tonight1AM.toISOString(), 146: delayMs: delay, 147: }, 'One-time job scheduled for tonight at 1 AM'); 148: setTimeout(async () => { 149: logger.info('One-time job triggered'); 150: try { 151: await this.runBugfixAudit(); 152: } catch (error) { 153: logError(logger, error, 'One-time bugfix audit failed'); 154: } 155: }, delay); 156: } 157: } 158: if (import.meta.url === `file://${process.argv[1]}`) { 159: const args = process.argv.slice(2); 160: const runNow = args.includes('--run-now') || args.includes('--now'); 161: const once = args.includes('--once') || args.includes('--one-time'); 162: const recurring = args.includes('--recurring') || args.includes('--cron'); 163: let schedule: string | null = null; 164: const scheduleIndex = args.indexOf('--schedule'); 165: if (scheduleIndex !== -1 && args[scheduleIndex + 1]) { 166: schedule = args[scheduleIndex + 1]; 167: } 168: const pipeline = new BugfixAuditPipeline(); 169: if (runNow) { 170: logger.info('Running bugfix audit immediately'); 171: pipeline.runBugfixAudit() 172: .then((result) => { 173: logger.info({ result }, 'Bugfix audit completed'); 174: process.exit(0); 175: }) 176: .catch((error: unknown) => { 177: logError(logger, error, 'Bugfix audit failed'); 178: process.exit(1); 179: }); 180: } else if (recurring) { 181: const cronSchedule = schedule ?? '0 1 * * *'; 182: logStart(logger, 'bugfix audit pipeline in scheduled mode', { cronSchedule }); 183: pipeline.scheduleAudits(cronSchedule); 184: logger.info('Bugfix audit pipeline running. Press Ctrl+C to stop.'); 185: } else if (once) { 186: pipeline.scheduleTonight(); 187: logger.info('One-time execution scheduled. Process will keep running.'); 188: } else { 189: pipeline.scheduleTonight(); 190: logger.info('No flags specified — defaulting to one-time tonight at 1 AM.'); 191: } 192: } 193: export { BugfixAuditPipeline }; 100: this.setupEventListeners(); 101: } 102: private setupEventListeners(): void { 103: this.worker.on('job:created', (job: Job) => { 104: logger.info({ jobId: job.id }, 'Health check job created'); 105: }); 106: this.worker.on('job:started', (job: Job) => { 107: logger.info({ jobId: job.id }, 'Health check job started'); 108: }); 109: this.worker.on('job:completed', (job: Job) => { 110: const result = job.result as unknown as HealthCheckResult; 119: this.displayResults(result); 120: }); 121: this.worker.on('job:failed', (job: Job) => { 122: logError(logger, job.error, 'Health check job failed', { jobId: job.id }); 123: }); 124: } 125: private displayResults(result: HealthCheckResult): void { 126: printSummary(result); 127: if (result.recommendations.length > 0) { 128: printRecommendations(result.recommendations); 129: } 130: if (this.options.detailed) { 131: printDetailedChecks(result.checks); 132: } 133: } 134: async runHealthCheck(options: HealthCheckOptions = {}): Promise<JobStats> { 135: logStart(logger, 'health check', { options }); 69: try { 70: if (targetPath) { 71: const resolvedPath = path.resolve(targetPath); 72: worker.queueProject(resolvedPath); 73: } else { 74: const scanner = new DirectoryScanner({ 75: baseDir: CODE_BASE_DIR, 76: maxDepth: 2, 77: excludeDirs: [ 78: 'node_modules', 79: '.git', 80: 'dist', 81: 'build', 82: 'coverage', 83: '__pycache__', 84: '.venv', 85: 'venv' 86: ] 87: }); 203: } 204: if (schemaType === 'SoftwareApplication') { 205: impact.richResultsEligibility.push('Software app rich results'); 206: } 207: if (schemaType === 'TechArticle') { 208: impact.richResultsEligibility.push('Article rich results'); 240: } 241: } 242: // Generate recommendations 243: if (result.patterns.renderWaitFor > 5) { 244: result.recommendations.push('Create renderAndWait helper to reduce render + waitFor boilerplate'); 245: } 246: if (result.patterns.linkValidation > 5) { 247: result.recommendations.push('Create link assertion helpers (expectExternalLink, expectInternalLink, etc.)'); 248: } 249: if (result.patterns.semanticChecks > 5) { 250: result.recommendations.push('Create semantic validators (expectSectionWithId, expectHeadingLevel, etc.)'); 251: } 252: if (result.patterns.formInteractions > 5) { 253: result.recommendations.push('Create form helpers (fillContactForm, expectFormAccessibility, etc.)'); 254: } 255: if (result.patterns.hardcodedStrings.length > 10) { 256: result.recommendations.push('Extract hardcoded strings to test-constants.ts'); 257: } 258: return result; 259: } 260: /** 261: * Generate utility files based on analysis 262: */ 263: async generateUtilityFiles( 264: projectPath: string, 265: utilsDir: string, 266: e2eDir: string, 267: framework: string, 268: analysis: Analysis 269: ): Promise<string[]> { 270: const utilsPath = path.join(projectPath, utilsDir); 271: const generatedFiles: string[] = []; 272: // Ensure utils directory exists 273: await fs.mkdir(utilsPath, { recursive: true }); 274: // Generate assertions.ts 275: if (analysis.patterns.linkValidation > 0) { 276: const assertionsPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'assertions.ts'); 277: if (!await this.fileExists(assertionsPath)) { 278: await fs.writeFile(assertionsPath, this.generateAssertionsContent(framework)); 279: generatedFiles.push('assertions.ts'); 280: } 281: } 282: // Generate semantic-validators.ts 283: if (analysis.patterns.semanticChecks > 0) { 284: const validatorsPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'semantic-validators.ts'); 285: if (!await this.fileExists(validatorsPath)) { 286: await fs.writeFile(validatorsPath, this.generateSemanticValidatorsContent(framework)); 287: generatedFiles.push('semantic-validators.ts'); 288: } 289: } 290: // Generate form-helpers.ts 291: if (analysis.patterns.formInteractions > 0) { 292: const formHelpersPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'form-helpers.ts'); 293: if (!await this.fileExists(formHelpersPath)) { 294: await fs.writeFile(formHelpersPath, this.generateFormHelpersContent()); 295: generatedFiles.push('form-helpers.ts'); 296: } 297: } 298: // Generate test-constants.ts 299: if (analysis.patterns.hardcodedStrings.length > 0) { 300: const constantsPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'test-constants.ts'); 301: if (!await this.fileExists(constantsPath)) { 302: await fs.writeFile(constantsPath, this.generateConstantsContent(analysis.patterns.hardcodedStrings)); 303: generatedFiles.push('test-constants.ts'); 304: } 305: } 306: // Generate index.ts 307: const indexPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'index.ts'); 308: if (!await this.fileExists(indexPath) && generatedFiles.length > 0) { 309: await fs.writeFile(indexPath, this.generateIndexContent(generatedFiles)); 310: generatedFiles.push('index.ts'); 311: } 312: // Generate E2E fixtures if e2e directory exists 313: const e2ePath = path.join(projectPath, e2eDir); 314: if (await this.fileExists(e2ePath)) { 315: const fixturesPath = path.join(e2ePath, 'fixtures'); 316: await fs.mkdir(fixturesPath, { recursive: true }); 317: const navFixturesPath = path.join(fixturesPath, 'navigation.ts'); 318: if (!await this.fileExists(navFixturesPath)) { 319: await fs.writeFile(navFixturesPath, this.generateE2EFixturesContent()); 320: generatedFiles.push('e2e/fixtures/navigation.ts'); 321: } 322: } 323: return generatedFiles; 324: } 325: /** 326: * Check if file exists 327: */ 328: async fileExists(filePath: string): Promise<boolean> { 329: try { 330: await fs.access(filePath); 331: return true; 332: } catch { 333: return false; 334: } 335: } 336: /** 337: * Generate assertions.ts content 338: */ 339: generateAssertionsContent(framework: string): string { 340: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 341: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 342: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 343: return `/** 344: * Test Assertions Utilities 345: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 346: */ 347: ${importStatement} 348: export function expectExternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 349: const link = element.closest('a'); 350: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 351: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('target', '_blank'); 352: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer'); 353: } 354: export function expectInternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 355: const link = element.closest('a'); 356: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 357: } 358: export function expectMailtoLink(element: HTMLElement, email: string) { 359: const link = element.closest('a'); 360: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', \`mailto:\${email}\`); 361: } 362: export function expectSectionLink(element: HTMLElement, sectionId: string) { 363: expectInternalLink(element, \`#\${sectionId}\`); 364: } 365: export function expectAriaLabel(element: HTMLElement, label: string) { 366: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', label); 367: } 368: `; 369: } 370: /** 371: * Generate semantic-validators.ts content 372: */ 373: generateSemanticValidatorsContent(framework: string): string { 374: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 375: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 376: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 377: return `/** 378: * Semantic Validation Utilities 379: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 380: */ 381: import { screen } from '@testing-library/react'; 382: ${importStatement} 383: export function expectSectionWithId(id: string) { 384: const section = document.querySelector(\`section#\${id}\`); 385: expect(section).toBeInTheDocument(); 386: return section; 387: } 388: export function expectHeadingLevel(level: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6, name: string) { 389: const heading = screen.getByRole('heading', { level, name }); 390: expect(heading).toBeInTheDocument(); 391: return heading; 392: } 393: export function expectArticleCount(count: number) { 394: const articles = screen.getAllByRole('article'); 395: expect(articles).toHaveLength(count); 396: return articles; 397: } 398: export function expectContentInfo() { 399: const footer = screen.getByRole('contentinfo'); 400: expect(footer).toBeInTheDocument(); 401: return footer; 402: } 403: export function expectHeadingCount(level: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6, count: number) { 404: const headings = screen.getAllByRole('heading', { level }); 405: expect(headings).toHaveLength(count); 406: return headings; 407: } 408: export function expectListCount(minCount: number) { 409: const lists = screen.getAllByRole('list'); 410: expect(lists.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(minCount); 411: return lists; 412: } 413: export function expectImageWithAlt(altText: string) { 414: const img = screen.getByAltText(altText); 415: expect(img).toBeInTheDocument(); 416: return img; 417: } 418: `; 419: } 420: /** 421: * Generate form-helpers.ts content 422: */ 423: generateFormHelpersContent(): string { 424: return `/** 425: * Form Testing Utilities 426: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 427: */ 428: import { screen, within, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'; 429: import { getFormInputs(form: HTMLElement) { 435: return within(form).getAllByRole('textbox'); 436: } 437: export function getSubmitButton(form: HTMLElement) { 438: return within(form).getByRole('button', { name: /send|submit/i }); 439: } 440: export async function fillContactForm( 441: user: UserEvent, 442: data: { name: string; email: string; organization?: string; message?: string } 443: ) { 444: const form = getForm(); 445: const inputs = getFormInputs(form); 446: await user.type(inputs[0], data.name); 447: await user.type(inputs[1], data.email); 448: if (data.organization && inputs[2]) { 449: await user.type(inputs[2], data.organization); 450: } 451: return { form, inputs }; 452: } 453: export function expectFormAccessibility(form: HTMLElement) { 454: expect(form).toHaveAttribute('noValidate'); 455: expect(form).toHaveAttribute('aria-describedby'); 456: } 457: export async function waitForForm() { 458: await waitFor(() => { 459: expect(screen.getByRole('form')).toBeInTheDocument(); 460: }); 461: return getForm(); 462: } 463: `; 464: } 465: /** 466: * Categorize strings into meaningful groups based on content patterns 467: */ 468: categorizeStrings(strings: string[]): { 469: navigation: string[]; 470: actions: string[]; 471: accessibility: string[]; 472: messages: string[]; 473: headings: string[]; 474: labels: string[]; 475: misc: string[]; 476: } { 477: const categories = { 478: navigation: [] as string[], 479: actions: [] as string[], 480: accessibility: [] as string[], 481: messages: [] as string[], 482: headings: [] as string[], 483: labels: [] as string[], 484: misc: [] as string[] 485: }; 486: const navPatterns = /^(home|about|contact|services|portfolio|blog|faq|help|login|signup|sign up|sign in|register|logout|sign out|menu|nav)/i; 487: const actionPatterns = /^(submit|cancel|save|delete|edit|update|add|remove|create|confirm|continue|next|back|previous|send|close|open|reset|clear|apply|done)/i; 488: const a11yPatterns = /(open menu|close menu|toggle|expand|collapse|skip to|screen reader|aria|accessibility|keyboard|focus)/i; 489: const messagePatterns = /(error|success|warning|info|failed|invalid|required|please|thank you|completed|saved|deleted|updated|loading|processing)/i; 490: const headingPatterns = /^(welcome|get started|our |the |why |how |what |about us|contact us|frequently asked)/i; 491: const labelPatterns = /^(name|email|phone|address|password|username|message|subject|company|title|description|date|time|location|price|quantity|total)$/i; 492: for (const str of strings) { 493: const trimmed = str.trim(); 494: if (!trimmed) continue; 495: if (navPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 496: categories.navigation.push(trimmed); 497: } else if (actionPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 498: categories.actions.push(trimmed); 499: } else if (a11yPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 500: categories.accessibility.push(trimmed); 501: } else if (messagePatterns.test(trimmed)) { 502: categories.messages.push(trimmed); 503: } else if (headingPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 504: categories.headings.push(trimmed); 505: } else if (labelPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 506: categories.labels.push(trimmed); 507: } else { 508: categories.misc.push(trimmed); 509: } 510: } 511: return categories; 512: } 513: /** 514: * Generate a constant export for an array of strings 515: */ 516: generateArrayExport(name: string, strings: string[], comment: string): string { 517: if (strings.length === 0) return ''; 518: const escaped = strings.map(s => ` '${s.replace(/'/g, "\\'")}',`).join('\n'); 519: return ` 520: } 521: generateConstantsContent(hardcodedStrings: string[]): string { 522: const uniqueStrings = [...new Set(hardcodedStrings)].slice(0, 50); 523: const categories = this.categorizeStrings(uniqueStrings); 524: const sections: string[] = []; 525: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('NAV_STRINGS', categories.navigation, 'Navigation labels')); 526: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('ACTION_STRINGS', categories.actions, 'Button and action labels')); 527: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('A11Y_STRINGS', categories.accessibility, 'Accessibility labels')); 528: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('MESSAGE_STRINGS', categories.messages, 'User-facing messages')); 529: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('HEADING_STRINGS', categories.headings, 'Headings and titles')); 530: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('LABEL_STRINGS', categories.labels, 'Form field labels')); 531: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('MISC_STRINGS', categories.misc, 'Other extracted strings')); 532: const nonEmpty = sections.filter(s => s.length > 0).join('\n'); 533: return `/** 534: * Test Constants 535: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 536: * 537: * Auto-categorized by string content patterns. 538: */ 539: ${nonEmpty} 540: // Combined export for convenience 541: export const ALL_STRINGS = [ 542: ...NAV_STRINGS, 543: ...ACTION_STRINGS, 544: ...A11Y_STRINGS, 545: ...MESSAGE_STRINGS, 546: ...HEADING_STRINGS, 547: ...LABEL_STRINGS, 548: ...MISC_STRINGS, 549: ] as const; 550: `; 551: } 552: generateIndexContent(generatedFiles: string[]): string { 553: const exports = generatedFiles 554: .filter(f => f !== 'index.ts' && !f.includes('/')) 555: .map(f => `export * from './${f.replace('.ts', '')}';`) 556: .join('\n'); 557: return `/** 558: * Test Utilities Index 559: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 560: */ 561: ${exports} 562: `; 563: } 564: It provides event-driven job management with SQLite persistence, Sentry integration, and Git workflow automation. 15: 16: ## Architecture 17: 18: ``` 19: sidequest/ 20: ├── core/ # Base framework components 21: │ ├── server.js # SidequestServer base class (job queue engine) 22: │ ├── database.js # SQLite persistence layer 23: │ ├── config.js # Centralized configuration 24: │ └── index.js # Module exports 25: │ 26: ├── pipeline-core/ # Business logic for pipelines 27: │ ├── cache/ # Git-aware caching (Redis integration) 28: │ ├── config/ # Repository configuration loader 29: │ ├── errors/ # Error classification & retry logic 30: │ ├── extractors/ # Python code block extraction 31: │ ├── git/ # Branch management, PR creation 32: │ ├── models/ # Python Pydantic models (CodeBlock, DuplicateGroup) 33: │ ├── reports/ # HTML/JSON/Markdown report generators 34: │ ├── scanners/ # AST pattern detection, repository scanning 35: │ ├── similarity/ # Multi-layer similarity algorithm (Python) 36: │ ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions 37: │ └── utils/ # Error helpers 38: │ 39: ├── pipeline-runners/ # Pipeline entry points 40: │ ├── duplicate-detection-pipeline.js # 7-stage duplicate detection 41: │ ├── claude-health-pipeline.js # Claude Code health checks 42: │ ├── git-activity-pipeline.js # Git activity reports 43: │ ├── gitignore-pipeline.js # .gitignore management 44: │ ├── repo-cleanup-pipeline.js # Repository cleanup 45: │ ├── schema-enhancement-pipeline.js # Schema.org injection 46: │ ├── plugin-management-pipeline.js # Plugin management 47: │ └── test-refactor-pipeline.ts # Test suite refactoring 48: │ 49: ├── workers/ # Worker implementations (extend SidequestServer) 50: │ ├── duplicate-detection-worker.js # Duplicate detection jobs 51: │ ├── claude-health-worker.js # Health check jobs 52: │ ├── git-activity-worker.js # Git activity jobs 53: │ ├── gitignore-worker.js # .gitignore jobs 54: │ ├── repo-cleanup-worker.js # Cleanup jobs 55: │ ├── repomix-worker.js # Repomix jobs 56: │ ├── schema-enhancement-worker.js # Schema jobs 57: │ └── test-refactor-worker.ts # Test refactor jobs 58: │ 59: ├── bug-fixes/ # Automated bug fixing workflow 60: │ ├── bugfix-audit-worker.js # Multi-stage bug fix orchestrator 61: │ ├── launch-tonight.sh # Launch script 62: │ └── index.js # Module exports 63: │ 64: ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions 65: │ └── duplicate-detection-types.ts # Zod schemas + types 66: │ 67: ├── utils/ # Utility modules 68: │ ├── logger.js # Pino component logger 69: │ ├── doppler-resilience.js # Doppler fallback handling 70: │ ├── directory-scanner.js # Directory scanning 71: │ ├── pipeline-names.js # Pipeline name constants 72: │ ├── plugin-manager.js # Plugin management 73: │ ├── report-generator.js # Report utilities 74: │ ├── gitignore-repomix-updater.js # Gitignore sync 75: │ ├── schema-mcp-tools.js # Schema MCP tools 76: │ └── refactor-test-suite.ts # Test refactoring utility 77: │ 78: └── config files 79: ├── .env.example # Environment template 80: ├── .gitignore # Git ignore rules 81: ├── git-report-config.json # Git report config 82: └── repomix.config.json # Repomix configuration 83: ``` 84: 85: ## Core Framework 86: 87: ### SidequestServer (core/server.js) 88: 89: The base class for all workers with: 90: 91: - **Event-driven lifecycle**: `created → queued → running → completed/failed` 92: - **Concurrency control**: Configurable max concurrent jobs (default: 3) 93: - **SQLite persistence**: Job history stored in `data/jobs.db` 94: - **Sentry integration**: Error tracking and performance monitoring 95: - **Git workflow**: Optional branch creation, commits, and PR automation 96: - **Auto-retry with circuit breaker**: Classifies errors as retryable/non-retryable 97: 98: ```javascript 99: import { SidequestServer } from './core/server.js'; 100: 101: class MyWorker extends SidequestServer { 102: constructor(options) { 103: super({ ...options, jobType: 'my-job-type' }); 104: } 105: 106: async runJobHandler(job) { 107: // Implement job logic 108: return { success: true }; 109: } 110: } 111: ``` 112: 113: ### Error Classification (pipeline-core/errors/error-classifier.js) 114: 115: Intelligent error handling with retry logic: 116: 117: - **Retryable**: ETIMEDOUT, ECONNRESET, 5xx HTTP errors, rate limits 118: - **Non-retryable**: ENOENT, EACCES, 4xx HTTP errors, validation errors 119: - **Suggested delays**: Rate limits (60s), timeouts (10s), connection errors (5s) 120: 121: ## Pipelines 122: 123: ### 1. Duplicate Detection Pipeline 124: 125: 7-stage pipeline combining JavaScript (stages 1-2) and Python (stages 3-7): 126: 127: ```bash 128: doppler run -- RUN_ON_STARTUP=true node sidequest/pipeline-runners/duplicate-detection-pipeline.js 129: ``` 130: 131: **Stages:** 132: 1. Repository scanning (JS) 133: 2. AST pattern detection with ast-grep (JS) 134: 3. Code block extraction (Python) 135: 4. Semantic annotation (Python) 136: 5. Multi-layer similarity calculation (Python) 137: 6. Duplicate grouping (Python) 138: 7. Report generation (HTML/JSON/Markdown) 139: 140: **Similarity Algorithm Layers:** 141: - Layer 0: Complexity filtering (trivial code exclusion) 142: - Layer 1: Exact matching (hash-based, O(n)) 143: - Layer 2: Structural similarity (AST-based, configurable threshold) 144: - Layer 3: Semantic validation (category, tags, method chains) 145: - Layer 4: Quality filtering (group quality score) 146: 147: ### 2. Claude Health Pipeline 148: 149: Health checks for Claude Code configuration: 150: 151: ```bash 152: npm run claude:health # Immediate check 153: npm run claude:health:detailed # With component details 154: npm run claude:health:schedule # Cron scheduler (daily 8 AM) 155: ``` 156: 157: **Checks:** 158: - Configuration validation (settings.json, skill-rules.json) 159: - Component inventory (skills, agents, hooks, plugins) 160: - Hook permissions and executability 161: - Performance log analysis (slow hooks, failures) 162: - Plugin duplicate detection 163: 164: ### 3. Git Activity Pipeline 165: 166: Weekly/monthly git activity reports: 167: 168: ```bash 169: python3 sidequest/pipeline-runners/collect_git_activity.py --weekly 170: python3 sidequest/pipeline-runners/collect_git_activity.py --days 30 171: ``` 172: 173: **Features:** 174: - Multi-repository scanning 175: - Language distribution analysis 176: - Commit frequency metrics 177: - Jekyll-formatted markdown output 178: 179: ### 4. Repository Cleanup Pipeline 180: 181: Automated cleanup of build artifacts and virtual environments: 182: 183: ```bash 184: npm run cleanup:dryrun # Preview changes 185: npm run cleanup:once # Execute cleanup 186: ``` 187: 188: **Targets:** 189: - Python venvs (.venv, __pycache__, *.pyc) 190: - Node modules (node_modules, .npm-cache) 191: - Build artifacts (dist, build, *.log) 192: - Temporary files (temp, tmp, *.swp) 193: 194: ### 5. Schema Enhancement Pipeline 195: 196: Inject Schema.org structured data into HTML documents. 197: 198: ### 6. GitIgnore Pipeline 199: 200: Sync and validate .gitignore files across repositories. 201: 202: ## Python Components 203: 204: Located in `pipeline-core/` with dependencies on: 205: - **Pydantic**: Data models (CodeBlock, DuplicateGroup, ScanReport) 206: - **Standard library**: hashlib, re, json, sys 207: 208: ### Models (pipeline-core/models/) 209: 210: ```python 211: from code_block import CodeBlock, SourceLocation 212: from duplicate_group import DuplicateGroup 213: from consolidation_suggestion import ConsolidationSuggestion 214: from scan_report import ScanReport, ScanMetrics 215: ``` 216: 217: ### Similarity Module (pipeline-core/similarity/) 218: 219: ```python 220: from similarity.structural import calculate_structural_similarity, normalize_code 221: from similarity.grouping import group_by_similarity 222: from similarity.semantic import are_semantically_compatible, validate_duplicate_group 223: ``` 224: 225: **Configuration** (via environment variables): 226: - `STRUCTURAL_THRESHOLD`: Minimum similarity (default: 0.90) 227: - `MIN_GROUP_QUALITY`: Group quality threshold (default: 0.70) 228: - `MIN_LINE_COUNT`: Minimum lines (default: 1) 229: - `MIN_UNIQUE_TOKENS`: Minimum tokens (default: 3) 230: 231: ## Configuration 232: 233: ### Environment Variables 234: 235: Managed via Doppler (`bottleneck` project): 236: 237: ```bash 238: doppler setup --project bottleneck --config dev 239: doppler run -- node sidequest/pipeline-runners/duplicate-detection-pipeline.js 240: ``` 241: 242: **Key variables:** 243: - `JOBS_API_PORT`: API server port (default: 8080) 244: - `SENTRY_DSN`: Sentry error tracking 245: - `ENABLE_GIT_WORKFLOW`: Enable branch/PR creation 246: - `ENABLE_PR_CREATION`: Auto-create PRs for changes 247: - `PIPELINE_DEBUG`: Enable verbose Python debug output 248: 249: ### Repository Configuration (config/scan-repositories.json) 250: 251: ```json 252: { 253: "repositories": [ 254: { 255: "name": "my-repo", 256: "path": "/path/to/repo", 257: "enabled": true, 258: "priority": "high", 259: "scanFrequency": "daily" 260: } 261: ], 262: "groups": [ 263: { 264: "name": "web-projects", 265: "repositories": ["repo1", "repo2"], 266: "scanType": "inter-project" 267: } 268: ] 269: } 270: ``` 271: 272: ## Reports 273: 274: Generated in multiple formats: 275: 276: ### HTML Reports 277: Interactive dashboards with: 278: - Metrics cards (code blocks, duplicate groups, LOC reduction) 279: - Distribution charts (by strategy, complexity) 280: - Duplicate group details with impact scores 281: - Consolidation suggestions with ROI scores 282: 283: ### JSON Reports 284: Machine-readable output for integration: 285: ```json 286: { 287: "scan_type": "intra-project", 288: "scan_metadata": { ... }, 289: "metrics": { ... }, 290: "duplicate_groups": [ ... ], 291: "suggestions": [ ... ] 292: } 293: ``` 294: 295: ### Markdown Reports 296: Human-readable summaries for documentation. 297: 298: ## Development 299: 300: ### Adding a New Worker 301: 302: 1. Create worker in `workers/`: 303: ```javascript 304: import { SidequestServer } from '../core/server.js'; 305: 306: export class MyWorker extends SidequestServer { 307: constructor(options = {}) { 308: super({ ...options, jobType: 'my-worker' }); 309: } 310: 311: async runJobHandler(job) { 312: // Job implementation 313: return { status: 'completed' }; 314: } 315: } 316: ``` 317: 318: 2. Create pipeline in `pipeline-runners/`: 319: ```javascript 320: import { MyWorker } from '../workers/my-worker.js'; 321: 322: const worker = new MyWorker({ maxConcurrent: 1 }); 323: worker.addJob({ data: 'example' }); 324: ``` 325: 326: ### Type Definitions 327: 328: Use Zod schemas with TypeScript inference: 329: ```typescript 330: import { z } from 'zod'; 331: 332: export const MyJobDataSchema = z.object({ 333: type: z.literal('my-job'), 334: target: z.string() 335: }).strict(); 336: 337: export type MyJobData = z.infer<typeof MyJobDataSchema>; 338: ``` 339: 340: ## Testing 341: 342: ```bash 343: npm test # Unit tests 344: npm run test:integration # Integration tests 345: npm run typecheck # TypeScript checks 346: ``` 347: 348: ## Related Documentation 349: 350: - `../docs/API_REFERENCE.md` - REST API endpoints 351: - `../docs/architecture/ERROR_HANDLING.md` - Retry logic details 352: - `../docs/architecture/TYPE_SYSTEM.md` - Type patterns 353: - 99: const age = await getFileAgeDays(fullPath); 100: const size = (await fs.stat(fullPath)).size; 101: archivedLogs.push({ 102: path: fullPath, 103: name: entry.name, 104: ageDays: age, 105: sizeBytes: size 106: }); 124: } 125: if (!dryRun) { 126: await fs.mkdir(archiveDir, { recursive: true }); 127: } 128: let archivedCount = 0; 129: let totalSize = 0; 130: for (const log of toArchive) { 131: const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/:/g, '-').split('.')[0]; 132: const archiveName = `${path.basename(log.name, '.json')}-${timestamp}.json.gz`; 133: const archivePath = path.join(archiveDir, archiveName); 134: if (dryRun) { 135: if (verbose) { 136: console.log(` [DRY RUN] Would archive: ${log.name} (${log.ageDays.toFixed(1)} days old)`); 137: } 138: } else { 139: try { 140: await compressFile(log.path, archivePath); 141: await fs.unlink(log.path); 142: if (verbose) { 143: console.log(` ✓ Archived: ${log.name} → ${archiveName}`); 144: } 145: archivedCount++; 146: totalSize += log.sizeBytes; 147: } catch (error) { 148: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to archive error log', { file: log.name }); 149: } 150: } 151: } 152: const action = dryRun ? '[DRY RUN] Would archive' : 'Archived'; 153: console.log(`${action} ${toArchive.length} error logs (${formatBytes(totalSize)})`); 154: return { archived: archivedCount, totalSize }; 155: } 156: async function deleteOldArchives(archivedLogs, retentionDays, dryRun, verbose) { 157: const toDelete = archivedLogs.filter(log => log.ageDays > retentionDays); 158: if (toDelete.length === 0) { 159: console.log('✓ No old archives need deletion'); 160: return { deleted: 0, totalSize: 0 }; 161: } 162: let deletedCount = 0; 163: let totalSize = 0; 164: for (const log of toDelete) { 165: if (dryRun) { 166: if (verbose) { 167: console.log(` [DRY RUN] Would delete: ${log.name} (${log.ageDays.toFixed(1)} days old)`); 168: } 169: } else { 170: try { 171: await fs.unlink(log.path); 172: if (verbose) { 173: console.log(` ✓ Deleted: ${log.name}`); 174: } 175: deletedCount++; 176: totalSize += log.sizeBytes; 177: } catch (error) { 178: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to delete archived log', { file: log.name }); 179: } 180: } 181: } 182: const action = dryRun ? '[DRY RUN] Would delete' : 'Deleted'; 183: console.log(`${action} ${toDelete.length} old archives (${formatBytes(totalSize)})`); 184: return { deleted: deletedCount, totalSize }; 185: } 186: function printCleanupSummary(archiveResult: { archived: number; totalSize: number }, deleteResult: { deleted: number; totalSize: number }, dryRun: boolean) { 187: console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(50)); 188: console.log('📊 Summary'); 189: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 190: console.log(`Error logs archived: ${archiveResult.archived}`); 191: console.log(`Old archives deleted: ${deleteResult.deleted}`); 192: console.log(`Total space reclaimed: ${formatBytes(archiveResult.totalSize + deleteResult.totalSize)}`); 193: if (dryRun) console.log('\n⚠️ This was a dry run - no files were modified'); 194: } 195: async function cleanup(options) { 196: console.log('\n🧹 Error Log Cleanup\n'); 197: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 198: console.log(`Retention period: ${options.retentionDays} days`); 199: console.log(`Archive retention: ${ARCHIVE_RETENTION_DAYS} days`); 200: console.log(`Mode: ${options.dryRun ? 208: console.log(` Old (>${options.retentionDays} days): ${errorLogs.length - recent}\n`); 209: } 210: const archiveResult = await archiveOldLogs(errorLogs, options.retentionDays, options.dryRun, options.verbose); operations: set[str] = set() 286: for compiled_pattern, op in _COMPILED_ALL_OPERATIONS: 287: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 288: operations.add(op) 289: return operations 290: def _extract_domains(self, code: str, tags: list[str]) -> set[str]: 291: domains: set[str] = set() 292: text = code + ' ' + ' '.join(tags) 293: for compiled_pattern, domain in _COMPILED_DOMAIN: 294: if compiled_pattern.search(text): 295: domains.add(domain) 296: return domains 297: def _extract_patterns(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 298: patterns: set[str] = set() 299: for compiled_pattern, name in _COMPILED_CODE_PATTERNS: 300: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 301: patterns.add(name) 302: return patterns 303: def _extract_data_types(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 304: data_types: set[str] = set() 305: for compiled_pattern, dtype in _COMPILED_DATA_TYPES: 306: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 307: data_types.add(dtype) 308: return data_types 309: def _infer_intent( 310: self, 311: operations: set[str], 312: domains: set[str], 313: patterns: set[str], 314: ) -> str: 315: parts: list[str] = [] 316: if operations: 317: parts.append('+'.join(sorted(operations))) 318: if domains: 319: parts.append('on:' + '+'.join(sorted(domains))) 320: if patterns: 321: parts.append('with:' + '+'.join(sorted(patterns))) 322: return '|'.join(parts) if parts else 'unknown' 1: import hashlib 2: 'total_lines': 9, 81: 'affected_files': ['src/utils.js', 'src/helpers.js'], 82: } 83: } 84: } 85: @field_validator('member_block_ids') 86: @classmethod 87: def validate_min_members(cls, v): 88: if len(v) < 2: 89: raise ValueError('A duplicate group must have at least 2 members') 90: return v 91: @field_validator('canonical_block_id') 92: @classmethod 93: def validate_canonical_in_members(cls, v, info): 94: if v is not None and 'member_block_ids' in info.data: 95: if v not in info.data['member_block_ids']: 96: raise ValueError('canonical_block_id must be one of member_block_ids') 97: return v 98: @computed_field 99: @property 100: def deduplication_potential(self) -> int: 101: if self.occurrence_count <= 1: 102: return 0 103: avg_lines_per_instance = self.total_lines / self.occurrence_count 104: return int((self.occurrence_count - 1) * avg_lines_per_instance) 105: @computed_field 106: @property 107: def impact_score(self) -> float: 108: occurrence_factor = min(self.occurrence_count / ImpactWeights.OCCURRENCE_CAP, 1.0) 109: similarity_factor = self.similarity_score 110: loc_factor = min(self.total_lines / ImpactWeights.LOC_CAP, 1.0) 111: score = ( 112: occurrence_factor * ImpactWeights.OCCURRENCE + 113: similarity_factor * ImpactWeights.SIMILARITY + 114: loc_factor * ImpactWeights.SIZE 115: ) 116: return round(score, 2) 117: @computed_field 118: @property 119: def is_cross_repository(self) -> bool: 120: return len(self.affected_repositories) > 1 121: @computed_field 122: @property 123: def priority_level(self) -> str: 124: if block_id in self.member_block_ids: 139: self.member_block_ids.remove(block_id) 140: self.occurrence_count = len(self.member_block_ids) 141: self.updated_at = datetime.utcnow() 142: if self.canonical_block_id == block_id: 143: self.canonical_block_id = None 144: def set_canonical(self, block_id: str) -> None: 145: if block_id not in self.member_block_ids: 146: raise ValueError(f"Block {block_id} is not a member of this group") 147: self.canonical_block_id = block_id 148: self.updated_at = datetime.utcnow() 149: def __hash__(self) -> int: 150: return utilsDir: 'tests/utils', 27: e2eDir: 'tests/e2e', 28: framework: 'vitest' 29: }; 30: function detectFramework(projectPath: string): 'vitest' | 'jest' | 'playwright' { 31: const packageJsonPath = path.join(projectPath, 'package.json'); 32: if (!fs.existsSync(packageJsonPath)) { 33: return 'vitest'; 34: } 35: const packageJson = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(packageJsonPath, 'utf-8')); 36: const deps = { ...packageJson.dependencies, ...packageJson.devDependencies }; 37: if (deps['vitest']) return 'vitest'; 38: if (deps['@playwright/test']) return 'playwright'; 39: return 'jest'; 40: } 41: async function findTestFiles(config: RefactorConfig): Promise<string[]> { 42: const patterns = [ 43: `${config.testsDir}/**/*.test.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, 44: `${config.testsDir}/**/*.spec.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, 45: `src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, 46: `src/**/*.spec.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}` 47: ]; 48: const files: string[] = []; 49: for (const pattern of patterns) { 50: const matches = await glob(pattern, { 51: cwd: config.projectPath, 52: ignore: ['**/node_modules/**'] 53: }); 54: files.push(...matches); 55: } 56: return [...new Set(files)]; 57: } 58: async function analyzeTestFiles(config: RefactorConfig, testFiles: string[]): Promise<AnalysisResult> { 59: const result: AnalysisResult = { 60: testFiles, 61: patterns: { 62: renderWaitFor: 0, 63: linkValidation: 0, 64: semanticChecks: 0, 65: formInteractions: 0, 66: hardcodedStrings: [], 67: duplicateAssertions: [] 68: }, 69: recommendations: [] 70: }; 71: const stringCounts = new Map<string, number>(); 72: const assertionCounts = new Map<string, number>(); 73: for (const file of testFiles) { 74: const filePath = path.join(config.projectPath, file); 75: const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8'); 76: const renderWaitMatches = content.match(/render\s*\([^)]+\);\s*await\s+waitFor/g); 77: result.patterns.renderWaitFor += renderWaitMatches?.length || 0; 78: const linkMatches = content.match(/toHaveAttribute\s*\(\s*['"]href['"]/g); 79: result.patterns.linkValidation += linkMatches?.length || 0; 80: // Count semantic checks 81: const semanticMatches = content.match(/getByRole\s*\(\s*['"]heading['"]|querySelector\s*\(\s*['"]section#/g); 82: result.patterns.semanticChecks += semanticMatches?.length || 0; 83: // Count form interactions 84: const formMatches = content.match(/userEvent\.type|fireEvent\.click|getByRole\s*\(\s*['"]form['"]/g); 85: result.patterns.formInteractions += formMatches?.length || 0; 86: // Extract hardcoded strings (quoted strings in expect statements) 87: const stringMatches = content.matchAll(/expect\s*\([^)]+\)\.toBeInTheDocument\(\)|getByText\s*\(\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]\)/g); 88: for (const match of stringMatches) { 89: if (match[1]) { 90: const count = stringCounts.get(match[1]) || 0; 91: stringCounts.set(match[1], count + 1); 92: } 93: } 94: // Extract assertion patterns 95: const assertionMatches = content.matchAll(/(expect\([^)]+\)\.[^;]+);/g); 96: for (const match of assertionMatches) { 97: const assertion = match[1].replace(/['"][^'"]+['"]/g, '""'); 98: const count = assertionCounts.get(assertion) || 0; 99: assertionCounts.set(assertion, count + 1); 100: } 101: } 102: // Find duplicated strings (appearing 3+ times) 103: for (const [str, count] of stringCounts) { 104: if (count >= 3 && str.length > 5) { 105: result.patterns.hardcodedStrings.push(str); 106: } 107: } 108: // Find duplicated assertions (appearing 3+ times) 109: for (const [assertion, count] of assertionCounts) { 110: if (count >= 3) { 111: result.patterns.duplicateAssertions.push(assertion); 112: } 113: } 114: // Generate recommendations 115: if (result.patterns.renderWaitFor > 5) { 116: result.recommendations.push('Create renderAndWait helper to reduce render + waitFor boilerplate'); 117: } 118: if (result.patterns.linkValidation > 5) { 119: result.recommendations.push('Create link assertion helpers (expectExternalLink, expectInternalLink, etc.)'); 120: } 121: if (result.patterns.semanticChecks > 5) { 122: result.recommendations.push('Create semantic validators (expectSectionWithId, expectHeadingLevel, etc.)'); 123: } 124: if (result.patterns.formInteractions > 5) { 125: result.recommendations.push('Create form helpers (fillContactForm, expectFormAccessibility, etc.)'); 126: } 127: if (result.patterns.hardcodedStrings.length > 10) { 128: result.recommendations.push('Extract hardcoded strings to test-constants.ts'); 129: } 130: if (result.patterns.duplicateAssertions.length > 5) { 131: result.recommendations.push('Extract duplicate assertions to custom assertion helpers'); 132: } 133: return result; 134: } 135: /** 136: * Generate the assertions utility file 137: */ 138: function generateAssertionsFile(framework: string): string { 139: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 140: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 141: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 142: return `/** 143: * Test Assertions Utilities 144: * 145: * Common assertion helpers for link validation and DOM element checks. 146: * Reduces duplication across component tests. 147: */ 148: ${importStatement} 149: /** 150: * Asserts that an element is an external link with proper security attributes 151: */ 152: export function expectExternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 153: const link = element.closest('a'); 154: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 155: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('target', '_blank'); 156: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer'); 157: } 158: /** 159: * Asserts that an element is an internal link with correct href 160: */ 161: export function expectInternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 162: const link = element.closest('a'); 163: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 164: } 165: /** 166: * Asserts that an element is a mailto link with correct email 167: */ 168: export function expectMailtoLink(element: HTMLElement, email: string) { 169: const link = element.closest('a'); 170: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', \`mailto:\${email}\`); 171: } 172: /** 173: * Asserts that an element is a tel link with correct phone number 174: */ 175: export function expectTelLink(element: HTMLElement, phone: string) { 176: const link = element.closest('a'); 177: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', \`tel:\${phone}\`); 178: } 179: /** 180: * Asserts that all external links in a container have proper security attributes 181: */ 182: export function expectAllExternalLinksSecure(container: HTMLElement) { 183: const links = container.querySelectorAll('a[target="_blank"]'); 184: links.forEach(link => { 185: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer'); 186: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href'); 187: }); 188: return links.length; 189: } 190: export function expectSectionLink(element: HTMLElement, sectionId: string) { 191: expectInternalLink(element, \`#\${sectionId}\`); 192: } 193: export function expectAriaLabel(element: HTMLElement, label: string) { 194: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', label); 195: } 196: export function expectAriaDescribedBy(element: HTMLElement) { 197: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('aria-describedby'); 198: } 199: export function expectButtonType(element: HTMLElement, type: 'submit' | 'button' | 'reset') { 200: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('type', type); 201: } 202: export function expectEnabled(element: HTMLElement) { 203: expect(element).not.toBeDisabled(); 204: } 205: export function expectDisabled(element: HTMLElement) { 206: expect(element).toBeDisabled(); 207: } 208: `; 209: } 210: /** 211: * Generate the semantic validators utility file 212: */ 213: function generateSemanticValidatorsFile(framework: string): string { 214: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 215: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 216: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 217: return ` 218: import { screen } from '@testing-library/react'; 219: ${importStatement} 220: export function expectSectionWithId(id: string) { 221: const section = document.querySelector(\`section#\${id}\`); 222: expect(section).toBeInTheDocument(); 223: return section; 224: } 225: export function expectHeadingLevel(level: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6, name: string) { 226: const heading = screen.getByRole('heading', { level, name }); 227: expect(heading).toBeInTheDocument(); 228: return heading; 229: } 230: export function expectArticleCount(count: number) { 231: const articles = screen.getAllByRole('article'); 232: expect(articles).toHaveLength(count); 233: return articles; 234: } 235: export function expectListItemCount(minCount: number) { 236: const listItems = screen.getAllByRole('listitem'); 237: expect(listItems.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(minCount); 261: expect(headings).toHaveLength(count); 262: return headings; 263: } 264: export function expectListCount(minCount: number) { 265: const lists = screen.getAllByRole('list'); 266: expect(lists.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(minCount); 267: return lists; 268: } 269: export function expectImageWithAlt(altText: string) { 270: const img = screen.getByAltText(altText); 271: expect(img).toBeInTheDocument(); 272: return img; 273: } 274: export function expectButton(name: string | RegExp) { 275: const button = screen.getByRole('button', { name }); 282: return link; 283: } 284: `; 285: } 286: /** 287: * Generate the form helpers utility file 288: */ 289: function generateFormHelpersFile(): string { 290: return ` 291: import { screen, within, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'; 292: import { expect } from 'vitest'; 293: import type { UserEvent } from '@testing-library/user-event'; 294: export function getForm() { 295: return screen.getByRole('form'); 296: } 297: export function getFormInputs(form: HTMLElement) { 298: return within(form).getAllByRole('textbox'); 337: expect(emailInput).toBeInTheDocument(); 338: if (emailInput) { 339: expect(emailInput).toHaveAttribute('type', 'email'); 340: expect(emailInput).toHaveAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); 341: } 342: return emailInput; 343: } 344: export function expectInputsHaveDescriptions(inputs: HTMLElement[]) { 345: const inputsWithDescriptions = inputs.filter(input => 346: input.hasAttribute('aria-describedby') 347: ); 348: expect(inputsWithDescriptions.length).toBeGreaterThan(0); 349: return inputsWithDescriptions; 350: } 351: export function expectFormValues( 352: inputs: HTMLElement[], 353: expected: { name?: string; email?: string; organization?: string } 354: ) { 355: if (expected.name) { 356: expect(inputs[0]).toHaveValue(expected.name); 384: labels: string[]; 385: misc: string[]; 386: } 387: /** 388: * Categorize strings into meaningful groups based on content patterns 389: */ 390: function categorizeStrings(strings: string[]): CategorizedStrings { 391: const categories: CategorizedStrings = { 392: navigation: [], 393: actions: [], 394: accessibility: [], 395: messages: [], 396: headings: [], 397: labels: [], 398: misc: [] 399: }; 400: const navPatterns = /^(home|about|contact|services|portfolio|blog|faq|help|login|signup|sign up|sign in|register|logout|sign out|menu|nav)/i; 401: const actionPatterns = /^(submit|cancel|save|delete|edit|update|add|remove|create|confirm|continue|next|back|previous|send|close|open|reset|clear|apply|done)/i; 402: const a11yPatterns = /(open menu|close menu|toggle|expand|collapse|skip to|screen reader|aria|accessibility|keyboard|focus)/i; 403: const messagePatterns = /(error|success|warning|info|failed|invalid|required|please|thank you|completed|saved|deleted|updated|loading|processing)/i; 404: const headingPatterns = /^(welcome|get started|our |the |why |how |what |about us|contact us|frequently asked)/i; 405: const labelPatterns = /^(name|email|phone|address|password|username|message|subject|company|title|description|date|time|location|price|quantity|total)$/i; 406: for (const str of strings) { 407: const trimmed = str.trim(); 408: if (!trimmed) continue; 409: if (navPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 410: categories.navigation.push(trimmed); 411: } else if (actionPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 412: categories.actions.push(trimmed); 413: } else if (a11yPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 414: categories.accessibility.push(trimmed); 415: } else if (messagePatterns.test(trimmed)) { 416: categories.messages.push(trimmed); 417: } else if (headingPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 418: categories.headings.push(trimmed); 419: } else if (labelPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 420: categories.labels.push(trimmed); 421: } else { 422: categories.misc.push(trimmed); 423: } 424: } 425: return categories; 426: } 427: /** 428: * Generate a constant export for an array of strings 429: */ 430: function generateArrayExport(name: string, strings: string[], comment: string): string { 431: if (strings.length === 0) return ''; 432: const escaped = strings.map(s => ` '${s.replace(/'/g, "\\'")}',`).join('\n'); 433: return `// ${comment}\nexport const ${name} = [\n${escaped}\n] as const;\n`; 434: } 435: function generateConstantsTemplate(hardcodedStrings: string[]): string { 436: const uniqueStrings = [...new Set(hardcodedStrings)].slice(0, 50); 437: const categories = categorizeStrings(uniqueStrings); 438: const sections: string[] = []; 439: sections.push(generateArrayExport('NAV_STRINGS', categories.navigation, 'Navigation labels')); 440: sections.push(generateArrayExport('ACTION_STRINGS', categories.actions, 'Button and action labels')); 441: sections.push(generateArrayExport('A11Y_STRINGS', categories.accessibility, 'Accessibility labels')); 442: sections.push(generateArrayExport('MESSAGE_STRINGS', categories.messages, 'User-facing messages')); 443: sections.push(generateArrayExport('HEADING_STRINGS', categories.headings, 'Headings and titles')); 444: sections.push(generateArrayExport('LABEL_STRINGS', categories.labels, 'Form field labels')); 445: sections.push(generateArrayExport('MISC_STRINGS', categories.misc, 'Other extracted strings')); 446: const nonEmpty = sections.filter(s => s.length > 0).join('\n'); 447: return `/** 448: * Test Constants 449: * 450: * Centralized content strings and expected data for tests. 451: * Makes tests more maintainable when content changes. 452: * Auto-categorized by string content patterns. 453: */ 454: ${nonEmpty} 455: // Combined export for convenience 456: export const ALL_STRINGS = [ 457: ...NAV_STRINGS, 458: ...ACTION_STRINGS, 459: ...A11Y_STRINGS, 460: ...MESSAGE_STRINGS, 461: ...HEADING_STRINGS, 462: ...LABEL_STRINGS, 463: ...MISC_STRINGS, 464: ] as const; 465: `; 466: } 467: function generateRenderHelpers(): string { 468: return ` 469: // ============================================================================ 470: // Render Helpers - Add these to your existing test-utils file 471: // ============================================================================ 472: /** 473: * Renders a component and waits for assertions to pass 474: * Reduces boilerplate in tests that follow render + waitFor pattern 475: */ 476: export async function renderAndWait( 477: ui: React.ReactElement, 478: assertions: () => void 479: ): Promise<void> { 480: const { waitFor } = await import('@testing-library/react'); 636: console.log(` Duplicate assertions: ${analysis.patterns.duplicateAssertions.length}`); 637: console.log(); 638: if (analysis.recommendations.length > 0) { 639: console.log('Recommendations:'); 640: console.log('────────────────'); 641: analysis.recommendations.forEach((rec, i) => console.log(` ${i + 1}. ${rec}`)); 642: console.log(); 643: } 644: } 645: function generateUtilityFiles(config: RefactorConfig, analysis: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof analyzeTestFiles>>, utilsPath: string) { 646: if (!fs.existsSync(utilsPath)) { 647: fs.mkdirSync(utilsPath, { recursive: true }); @field_validator('impact_score') 142: @classmethod 143: def round_impact_score(cls, v): 144: return round(v, 2) 145: @computed_field 146: @property 147: def priority(self) -> str: 148: if self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.CRITICAL and self.complexity in ['trivial', 'simple']: 149: return 'critical' 150: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.HIGH and self.complexity in ['trivial', 'simple', 'moderate']: 151: return 'high' 152: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.MEDIUM: 153: return 'medium' 154: else: 155: return 'low' 156: @computed_field 157: @property 158: def roi_score(self) -> float: 159: complexity_hours = { 160: ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL: EffortEstimates.TRIVIAL, 161: ImplementationComplexity.SIMPLE: EffortEstimates.SIMPLE, 162: ImplementationComplexity.MODERATE: EffortEstimates.MODERATE, 163: ImplementationComplexity.COMPLEX: EffortEstimates.COMPLEX, 164: ImplementationComplexity.VERY_COMPLEX: EffortEstimates.VERY_COMPLEX, 165: } 166: effort = self.estimated_effort_hours or complexity_hours.get(self.complexity, SuggestionDefaults.DEFAULT_EFFORT_FALLBACK) 167: if effort == 0: 168: return ROIMultipliers.MAX_SCORE 169: roi = (self.impact_score / effort) * SuggestionDefaults.ROI_NORMALIZER 170: return min(round(roi, 2), ROIMultipliers.MAX_SCORE) 171: @computed_field 172: @property 173: def is_quick_win(self) -> bool: 174: return ( 175: self.impact_score >= SuggestionDefaults.QUICK_WIN_MIN_IMPACT and 176: self.complexity in [ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL, ImplementationComplexity.SIMPLE] and 177: self.migration_risk in [MigrationRisk.MINIMAL, MigrationRisk.LOW] 178: ) 179: def add_migration_step( 180: self, 181: description: str, 182: code_example: Optional[str] = None, 183: automated: bool = False, 184: estimated_time: Optional[str] = None 185: ) -> None: 186: step_number = len(self.migration_steps) + 1 187: step = MigrationStep( 188: step_number=step_number, 189: description=description, 190: code_example=code_example, 191: automated=automated, 192: estimated_time=estimated_time 193: ) 194: self.migration_steps.append(step) 195: def add_benefit(self, benefit: str) -> None: 196: if benefit not in self.benefits: 197: self.benefits.append(benefit) 198: def add_drawback(self, drawback: str) -> None: 199: if drawback not in self.drawbacks: 200: self.drawbacks.append(drawback) 201: **Complexity:** {self.complexity.value} 211: - **Risk:** {self.migration_risk.value} 212: - **LOC Reduction:** {self.loc_reduction or 'Unknown'} 213: - **Files Affected:** {self.affected_files_count} hash(self.suggestion_id) 222: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: 223: if not isinstance(other, ConsolidationSuggestion): 224: return NotImplemented 225: return self.suggestion_id == tags2 = set(block2.tags) 15: func1 = _extract_function_tag(tags1) 16: func2 = _extract_function_tag(tags2) 17: if func1 and func2: 18: if func1 == func2 and block1.location.file_path == set(block2.tags) 27: if not tags1 and not tags2: 28: return 1.0 29: if not tags1 or not tags2: 30: return SemanticWeights.PARTIAL_TAG_OVERLAP 31: intersection = tags1 & tags2 32: union = tags1 | tags2 33: return len(intersection) / len(union) if union else 0.0 34: def _extract_function_tag(tags: set) -> Optional[str]: 35: for tag in tags: 36: if tag.startswith(BlockExtraction.FUNCTION_TAG_PREFIX): 'total_duplicated_lines': 300, 81: 'potential_loc_reduction': 250, 82: 'duplication_percentage': 12.0, 83: 'total_suggestions': 12, 84: 'quick_wins': 5 85: } 86: } 87: } 88: } 89: @computed_field 90: @property 91: def is_multi_repository(self) -> bool: 92: return len(self.repositories) > 1 93: @computed_field 94: @property 95: def total_scanned_files(self) -> int: 96: return sum(repo.total_files for repo in self.repositories) 97: @computed_field 98: @property 99: def total_scanned_lines(self) -> int: 100: return sum(repo.total_lines for repo in self.repositories) 101: @computed_field 102: @property 103: def duplication_severity(self) -> str: 104: dup_pct = self.metrics.duplication_percentage 105: if dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.MINIMAL_PCT: 106: return 'minimal' 107: elif dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.LOW_PCT: 108: return 'low' 109: elif dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.MODERATE_PCT: 110: return 'moderate' 111: elif dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.HIGH_PCT: 112: return 'high' 113: else: 114: return 'critical' 115: @computed_field 116: @property 117: def consolidation_opportunity_score(self) -> float: 118: dup_factor = min(self.metrics.duplication_percentage / DuplicationThresholds.HIGH_PCT * 100, 100) 119: quick_win_factor = min(self.metrics.quick_wins / DuplicationThresholds.QUICK_WIN_CAP * 100, 100) 120: if self.total_scanned_lines > 0: 121: loc_reduction_factor = ( 122: self.metrics.potential_loc_reduction / self.total_scanned_lines * 100 123: ) 124: else: 125: loc_reduction_factor = 0 126: score = ( 127: dup_factor * OpportunityWeights.DUPLICATION + 128: quick_win_factor * OpportunityWeights.QUICK_WIN + 129: loc_reduction_factor * OpportunityWeights.LOC_REDUCTION 130: ) 131: return round(min(score, 100), 2) 132: 'total_files': self.total_scanned_files, 170: 'total_lines': self.total_scanned_lines, 171: 'duplication_percentage': self.metrics.duplication_percentage, 172: 'duplication_severity':
